---
title: "final_analysis"
output:
  pdf_document: default
---

# Introduction

One thing we realised is that the repeated CV classifier models (and the 80/20 split models) have a data leakage problem (see NMF_scaled_analysis.Rmd). This is because the CMFTS features used by the models are derived from NMF components, and the NMF model was trained on the entire dataset. This means that the resulting features in the test folds/sets are indirectly related to the entire dataset, so the models have essentially had a peek at the test data. This is likely to have inflated performance metrics. Even with the 80/20 split models, the split was performed after NMF, just before classification, which leads to the exact same issue.

Therefore, this Rmd script will be the final analysis and will address this problem in the following manner:

1.  Split data into 80/20 before NMF.
2.  Train the NMF model on the train set, then derive CMFTS features.
3.  Project NMF model onto the test set, then derive CMFTS features.
4.  Train classifiers (no CV) on the full training set features, evaluate on test set.
5.  Train classifiers using repeated CV, re-evaluate on test set.
6.  Test on 40 unseen videos from human experiment.

# Load Libraries & Data

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
```

```{r}
# Load data
# dta_nmf_input <- read.csv("dta_nmf_input.csv")

# Load data before smoothing and normalisation
dta_preprocessed <- read.csv("../../CSV files/OpenFace files/full_dataset_preprocessed.csv")
```

# Smooth Raw AU Data

Now that I am reading in the AU data before smoothing and normalisation, I will do the smoothing step here.

```{r}
dta_preprocessed_smth <- dta_preprocessed %>%
  group_by(video_filename) %>%
  mutate(across(contains("_r"), ~ zoo::rollmean(., k = 4, fill = "extend", align = "left"), .names = "smth_{col}")) %>%
  ungroup()
```

Visualise differences:

```{r}
# Visualise difference
dta_preprocessed_smth %>%
  filter(video_filename == "20__exit_phone_room") %>%
  select(frame, AU07_r, smth_AU07_r) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(AU07_r, smth_AU07_r), names_to = "Type", values_to = "Intensity") %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = frame, y = Intensity, color = Type)) +
  geom_line() +
  labs(
    title = "AU07 - Before vs. After Smoothing",
    x = "Frame",
    y = "AU Intensity"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```

# Split Data 80/20

First need to add the emotion column, easier to do that first:

```{r}
# Function to add emotion column
add_emotion_column <- function(df) {
  df %>%
    mutate(
      emotion = case_when(
        str_detect(video_filename, "_laughing") ~ "yes",
        str_detect(video_filename, "_happy") ~ "yes",
        str_detect(video_filename, "_serious") ~ "yes",
        str_detect(video_filename, "_angry") ~ "yes",
        str_detect(video_filename, "_disgusted") ~ "yes",
        TRUE ~ "no"
      ),
      emotion = factor(emotion, levels = c("yes", "no"))
    )
}

add_emotion_type_column <- function(df) {
  df %>%
    mutate(
      emotion_type = case_when(
        str_detect(video_filename, "_laughing")  ~ "laughing",
        str_detect(video_filename, "_happy")     ~ "happy",
        str_detect(video_filename, "_serious")   ~ "serious",
        str_detect(video_filename, "_angry")     ~ "angry",
        str_detect(video_filename, "_disgusted") ~ "disgusted",
        TRUE                                     ~ "neutral" # Use 'neutral' for the "no" case
      ),
      
      # Valence column
      valence = case_when(
        emotion_type %in% c("laughing", "happy") ~ "positive",
        emotion_type %in% c("angry", "disgusted") ~ "negative",
        emotion_type %in% c("serious", "neutral") ~ "neutral"
      ),
      
      emotion_type = factor(emotion_type),
      valence = factor(valence, levels = c("positive", "neutral", "negative"))
    )
}


dta_nmf_input <- add_emotion_column(dta_preprocessed_smth)
dta_nmf_input <- add_emotion_type_column(dta_nmf_input)
# Reorder columns
dta_nmf_input <- dta_nmf_input %>%
  select(video_filename, corresponding_video, scene_name, isfakeorreal, emotion, emotion_type, valence, frame, everything())
# Check order is correct
colnames(dta_nmf_input)
```

Now to create the train and test splits:

```{r}
set.seed(12345)

real_videos <- dta_nmf_input %>%
  filter(isfakeorreal == "real") %>%
  distinct(video_filename)

# Sample 80% of real videos for train set
train_real_videos <- sample(real_videos$video_filename,
                            size = floor(0.8 * nrow(real_videos)))

# Get corresponding fake videos for selected real videos
train_fake_videos <- dta_nmf_input %>%
  filter(video_filename %in% train_real_videos) %>%
  pull(corresponding_video) %>%
  unique()

# Train set
dta_nmf_input_train <- dta_nmf_input %>%
  filter(video_filename %in% c(train_real_videos, train_fake_videos))

# Test set is everything else
dta_nmf_input_test <- dta_nmf_input %>%
  filter(!video_filename %in% c(train_real_videos, train_fake_videos))


# Check distribution
print(length(unique(dta_nmf_input_train$video_filename)))
print(length(unique(dta_nmf_input_test$video_filename)))

# Check pairs are preserved
train_videos <- unique(dta_nmf_input_train$video_filename)
test_videos <- unique(dta_nmf_input_test$video_filename)

train_corresponding_videos <- dta_nmf_input %>%
  filter(video_filename %in% train_videos) %>%
  pull(corresponding_video) %>%
  unique()

if (length(intersect(train_corresponding_videos, test_videos)) == 0) {
  cat("Success.\n")
} else {
  cat("Error, the following pairs were split:\n")
  print(intersect(train_corresponding_videos, test_videos))
}
```

Training set: 370 videos

Test set: 94 videos

Additional checks confirmed the pairs are preserved and not split across train and test sets.

```{r}
# Save train data
write.csv(dta_nmf_input_train, "dta_nmf_input_train.csv", row.names = F)
```

# Balance Classes

First we need to see the distribution of classes in both train and test sets.

```{r}
# # Training set
# cat("Training set:\n")
# table(dta_nmf_input_train$isfakeorreal, dta_nmf_input_train$emotion)
# 
# # Test set
# cat("\nTest set:\n")
# table(dta_nmf_input_test$isfakeorreal, dta_nmf_input_test$emotion)


# Get actual number of unique videos in train set.
dta_nmf_input_train %>%
  distinct(video_filename, emotion) %>%
  count(emotion)

# Get actual number of unique videos in test set.
dta_nmf_input_test %>%
  distinct(video_filename, emotion) %>%
  count(emotion)

# Emotion types
dta_nmf_input_train %>%
  distinct(video_filename, emotion_type) %>%
  count(emotion_type)
dta_nmf_input_test %>%
  distinct(video_filename, emotion_type) %>%
  count(emotion_type)

# Valence
dta_nmf_input_train %>%
  distinct(video_filename, valence) %>%
  count(valence)
dta_nmf_input_test %>%
  distinct(video_filename, valence) %>%
  count(valence)
```

In the training set:

-   222 emotion

-   148 no emotion

There is a mild imbalance of emotion vs. no emotion videos (60/40). I could undersample here but it would be throwing away a lot of data. Instead I could oversample using something like SMOTE, although this would have to be done after CMFTS feature extraction as techniques like SMOTE are not really suitable for time series, high dimensional data like this.

UPDATE:

Upsampling cannot be used at this stage due to the complexity of the data, and also cannot be done at the CMFTS feature stage due to not having associated real/fake labels for the new data. Instead, downsampling will be the only available sampling option and will have to occur at this point before NMF. This will have to preserve video pairs too.

# Normalisation

This step will correctly apply normalisation to the videos, in a way that does not lose inter-video variation.

```{r}
au_cols <- names(dta_nmf_input_train)[grepl("^smth_AU", names(dta_nmf_input_train))]

# Learn scaling params from train set only
norm_params <- list()
for (col in au_cols) {
  mean_val <- mean(dta_nmf_input_train[[col]], na.rm = T)
  sd_val <- sd(dta_nmf_input_train[[col]], na.rm = T)
  
  # Handle AUs with zero SD
  if (is.na(sd_val) || sd_val == 0) {
    sd_val <- 1  # To prevent division by zero
  }
  
  norm_params[[col]] <- c(mean = mean_val, sd = sd_val)
}

# Create function to apply z-score and non-negative shift
fn_z_score_shift <- function(x, mean_val, sd_val) {
  z <- (x - mean_val) / sd_val
  # Shift for non-negativity
  z_shifted <- z - min(z, na.rm = T)
  z_shifted[is.na(z_shifted)] <- 0
  return(z_shifted)
}


# Apply params to both train and test sets
dta_nmf_input_train <- dta_nmf_input_train
dta_nmf_input_test <- dta_nmf_input_test

for (col in au_cols) {
  params <- norm_params[[col]]
  
  # Apply to train
  dta_nmf_input_train[[col]] <- fn_z_score_shift(dta_nmf_input_train[[col]],
                                                      params["mean"], params["sd"])
  dta_nmf_input_test[[col]] <- fn_z_score_shift(dta_nmf_input_test[[col]],
                                                      params["mean"], params["sd"])
}
```

Clean up columns:

```{r}
# Remove original AU columns + confidence, success, timestamp
dta_nmf_input_train <- dta_nmf_input_train[, -(9:28)]
dta_nmf_input_test <- dta_nmf_input_test[, -(9:28)]

# Rename AU cols
dta_nmf_input_train <- dta_nmf_input_train %>%
  rename(
    AU01_inner_brow_raiser = smth_AU01_r,
    AU02_outer_brow_raiser = smth_AU02_r,
    AU04_brow_lowerer = smth_AU04_r,
    AU05_upper_lid_raiser = smth_AU05_r,
    AU06_cheek_raiser = smth_AU06_r,
    AU07_lid_tightener = smth_AU07_r,
    AU09_nose_wrinkler = smth_AU09_r,
    AU10_upper_lip_raiser = smth_AU10_r,
    AU12_lip_corner_puller = smth_AU12_r,
    AU14_dimpler = smth_AU14_r,
    AU15_lip_corner_depressor = smth_AU15_r,
    AU17_chin_raiser = smth_AU17_r,
    AU20_lip_stretcher = smth_AU20_r,
    AU23_lip_tightener = smth_AU23_r,
    AU25_lips_part = smth_AU25_r,
    AU26_jaw_drop = smth_AU26_r,
    AU45_blink = smth_AU45_r
  )

dta_nmf_input_test <- dta_nmf_input_test %>%
  rename(
    AU01_inner_brow_raiser = smth_AU01_r,
    AU02_outer_brow_raiser = smth_AU02_r,
    AU04_brow_lowerer = smth_AU04_r,
    AU05_upper_lid_raiser = smth_AU05_r,
    AU06_cheek_raiser = smth_AU06_r,
    AU07_lid_tightener = smth_AU07_r,
    AU09_nose_wrinkler = smth_AU09_r,
    AU10_upper_lip_raiser = smth_AU10_r,
    AU12_lip_corner_puller = smth_AU12_r,
    AU14_dimpler = smth_AU14_r,
    AU15_lip_corner_depressor = smth_AU15_r,
    AU17_chin_raiser = smth_AU17_r,
    AU20_lip_stretcher = smth_AU20_r,
    AU23_lip_tightener = smth_AU23_r,
    AU25_lips_part = smth_AU25_r,
    AU26_jaw_drop = smth_AU26_r,
    AU45_blink = smth_AU45_r
  )
```

# NMF

```{r}
# Double check no negative values
any(as.matrix(t(dta_nmf_input[, 9:25])) < 0, na.rm = TRUE)
```

There are no negative values in the train or test sets.

## Optimal K

```{r, fig.height=2.5, fig.width=5}
# Installs required dependencies automatically and prevents update prompts
#remotes::install_github("zdebruine/RcppML", dependencies = TRUE, upgrade = "never")
set.seed(12345)

ranks <- 2:10
mse_values <- numeric(length(ranks))
names(mse_values) <- ranks

input_matrix <- as.matrix(t(dta_nmf_input_train[, 9:25]))

for (i in 1:length(ranks)) {
  k <- ranks[i]
  cat("Running RcppML NMF for rank =", k, "\n")
  res_rcppml <- tryCatch({
    RcppML::nmf(input_matrix,
                k = k,
                verbose = FALSE,
                tol = 1e-6,
                maxit = 750)
  }, error = function(e) {
    cat("Error running RcppML NMF for rank", k, ":", conditionMessage(e), "\n")
    return(NULL)
  })
  
  if (!is.null(res_rcppml)) {
    W <- res_rcppml$w
    H <- res_rcppml$h
    D <- res_rcppml$d
    V_hat <- W %*% diag(D) %*% H  # reconstruction
    
    # Caclulate RSS
    rss <- sum((input_matrix - V_hat)^2)
    # Calculate MSE
    mse_values[i] <- rss / (nrow(input_matrix) * ncol(input_matrix))
    cat("Rank", k, "completed. MSE:", mse_values[i], "\n")
    
  } else {
    mse_values[i] <- NA
  }
  rm(res_rcppml, W, H, V_hat); gc()
  
}

# Plot
dta_rank_metrics_mse <- data.frame(
  Rank = ranks,
  MSE = mse_values
)

plt_rank_estimation <- dta_rank_metrics_mse %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = Rank, y = MSE)) +
  geom_line(color = "blue") +
  geom_point(color = "blue", size = 3) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = dta_rank_metrics_mse$Rank) +
  labs(title = "NMF Rank Estimation: MSE vs Rank") +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5)
  )

plt_rank_estimation

ggsave(
  filename = "plt_rank_estimation.pdf",
  plot = plt_rank_estimation,
  height = 2.5,
  width = 5,
  device = cairo_pdf
)
```

## Run NMF

```{r}
set.seed(12345)
model_nmf <- RcppML::nmf(input_matrix, 
                         k = 3, 
                         tol = 1e-6, 
                         maxit = 750, 
                         verbose = TRUE)


# Extract H and scaling vector D
H <- model_nmf$h
D <- model_nmf$d

# Scale H matrix using d scaling vector
H_scaled <- sweep(H, 1, D, "*")

# Transpose H matrix to get correct row order
dta_h_matrix <- as.data.frame(t(H_scaled))

# Change column names
colnames(dta_h_matrix) <- paste0("nmf", 1:ncol(dta_h_matrix))

# Check row order in h_df matches row order in metadata
if(nrow(dta_h_matrix) != nrow(dta_nmf_input_train[, 2:8])) {
  stop("Number of samples in H matrix does not match number of rows in the metadata.")
}

# Add h to dta_nmf_input
dta_nmf_output <- dta_nmf_input_train %>%
  select(video_filename, corresponding_video, scene_name, isfakeorreal, emotion, emotion_type, valence, frame) %>%
  bind_cols(dta_h_matrix)
```

Save model:

```{r}
# Save NMF model
saveRDS(model_nmf, "model_nmf_trained.rds")
```

## Visualisations

```{r}
# Function for min-max normalisation, used in visualisations
fn_maxnormalise <- function(x, ...) {
  rng <- range(x, na.rm = TRUE)
  if (anyNA(rng) || diff(rng) == 0) {
    return(rep(0, length(x)))  # safe fallback: all 0s
  } else {
    return((x - rng[1]) / diff(rng))
  }
}
```

```{r, fig.width=8, fig.height=5}
library(RColorBrewer)

au_names <- c("AU01_inner_brow_raiser", "AU02_outer_brow_raiser", "AU04_brow_lowerer",
              "AU05_upper_lid_raiser", "AU06_cheek_raiser", "AU07_lid_tightener",
              "AU09_nose_wrinkler", "AU10_upper_lip_raiser", "AU12_lip_corner_puller",
              "AU14_dimpler", "AU15_lip_corner_depressor", "AU17_chin_raiser",
              "AU20_lip_stretcher", "AU23_lip_tightener", "AU25_lips_part",
              "AU26_jaw_drop", "AU45_blink")

rownames(model_nmf$w) <- au_names
colnames(model_nmf$w) <- c("nmf1", "nmf2", "nmf3")

# Basis matrix
NMF::aheatmap(
  model_nmf$w, 
  main = "Basis Matrix (W)", 
  color = colorRampPalette(brewer.pal(6, "Blues"))(100), 
  Rowv = NA, Colv = NA
)
# Normalise across rows for comparison
# Group by component for normalisation
NMF::aheatmap(
  apply(model_nmf$w, 2, fn_maxnormalise), 
  main = "Basis Matrix (W) - Normalised", 
  color = colorRampPalette(brewer.pal(6, "Blues"))(100), 
  scale = "none", 
  Rowv = NA, Colv = NA
)

apply(model_nmf$w, 2, fn_maxnormalise)

# Applied fix to normalised basis matrix by changing 1 to 2 (before it was normalising by rows not columns)

# # H matrix
# NMF::aheatmap(
#   H_scaled,
#   main = "Coefficient Matrix (H)",
#   color = viridis::magma(100),
#   Rowv = NA, Colv = NA
# )
# 
# NMF::aheatmap(
#   t(apply(H_scaled, 1, fn_maxnormalise)),
#   main = "Coefficient Matrix (H) - Normalised",
#   color = viridis::magma(100),
#   scale = "none",
#   Rowv = NA, Colv = NA
# )


# Save as PDF for paper
library(pdftools)
pdf("fig_nmf_basis_matrix_raw.pdf", width = 8, height = 5)
NMF::aheatmap(
  apply(model_nmf$w, 2, fn_maxnormalise), 
  main = "Basis Matrix (W) - Normalised", 
  color = colorRampPalette(brewer.pal(6, "Blues"))(100), 
  scale = "none", 
  Rowv = NA, Colv = NA
)
dev.off()
# Remove blank first page
pdf_subset("fig_nmf_basis_matrix_raw.pdf",
           pages = 2,
           output = "fig_nmf_basis_matrix.pdf")
```

The basis matrix heatmap is the same as before, which is good.

```{r}
dta_nmf_output %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = starts_with("nmf"),
    names_to = "Component",
    values_to = "Activation"
  ) %>%
  group_by(video_filename, Component) %>%
  mutate(Activation = fn_maxnormalise(Activation)) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  # Average across videos for each frame & component
  group_by(isfakeorreal, Component, frame) %>%
  summarise(Activation = mean(Activation, na.rm=TRUE)) %>%
  # Normalise again by component
  group_by(Component)%>%
  mutate(Activation = fn_maxnormalise(Activation))%>%
  ungroup() %>%
  # Plot heatmap
  ggplot( aes(x = frame, y = Component, fill = Activation)) +
  geom_tile() +
  facet_wrap(~ isfakeorreal, ncol = 2) +
  scale_fill_gradientn(colours = viridis::magma(100)) +
  labs(
    title = "Heatmap of Averaged NMF Component Activations",
    x = "Frame",
    y = "NMF Component",
    fill = "Activation"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(strip.text = element_text(size = 14)) 
```

```{r}
# Real vs. fake
dta_nmf_output %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = starts_with("nmf"),
    names_to = "Component",
    values_to = "Activation"
  ) %>%
  # Normalise within each video and component
  group_by(video_filename, Component) %>%
  mutate(Activation = fn_maxnormalise(Activation)) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  # Average across videos (by component and frame)
  group_by(isfakeorreal, Component, frame) %>%
  summarise(Activation = mean(Activation, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  # Normalise averaged values within each component
  group_by(Component) %>%
  mutate(Activation = fn_maxnormalise(Activation)) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  # Plot
  ggplot(aes(x = frame, y = Activation, colour = Component)) +
  geom_smooth() +
  facet_wrap(~ isfakeorreal, ncol = 1) +
  labs(
    title = "NMF Components Over Time (Real vs. Fake)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    strip.text = element_text(size = 14)  # Increase font size for facet labels
  )
```

All visualisations look similar to previous, suggesting everything is fine.

Separate real/fake basis matrix plots:

```{r}
set.seed(12345)
# --- Create REAL Video Matrix ---
# Filter for REAL videos from the training set
dta_nmf_input_train_real <- dta_nmf_input_train %>%
  filter(isfakeorreal == "real")

# Create the input matrix for REAL videos
# Note: Using the same column indices (9:25) as your original code
input_matrix_real <- as.matrix(t(dta_nmf_input_train_real[, 9:25]))

# --- Create FAKE Video Matrix ---
# Filter for FAKE videos from the training set
dta_nmf_input_train_fake <- dta_nmf_input_train %>%
  filter(isfakeorreal == "fake")

# Create the input matrix for FAKE videos
input_matrix_fake <- as.matrix(t(dta_nmf_input_train_fake[, 9:25]))


# --- Run NMF for REAL Videos ---
cat("Running NMF for REAL videos...\n")
model_nmf_real <- RcppML::nmf(input_matrix_real, 
                              k = 3, 
                              tol = 1e-6, 
                              maxit = 750, 
                              verbose = TRUE)

# --- Run NMF for FAKE Videos ---
cat("Running NMF for FAKE videos...\n")
model_nmf_fake <- RcppML::nmf(input_matrix_fake, 
                              k = 3, 
                              tol = 1e-6, 
                              maxit = 750, 
                              verbose = TRUE)

# Save the models (optional, but good practice)
saveRDS(model_nmf_real, "model_nmf_trained_REAL.rds")
saveRDS(model_nmf_fake, "model_nmf_trained_FAKE.rds")
```

```{r}
library(RColorBrewer)

au_names <- c("AU01_inner_brow_raiser", "AU02_outer_brow_raiser", "AU04_brow_lowerer",
              "AU05_upper_lid_raiser", "AU06_cheek_raiser", "AU07_lid_tightener",
              "AU09_nose_wrinkler", "AU10_upper_lip_raiser", "AU12_lip_corner_puller",
              "AU14_dimpler", "AU15_lip_corner_depressor", "AU17_chin_raiser",
              "AU20_lip_stretcher", "AU23_lip_tightener", "AU25_lips_part",
              "AU26_jaw_drop", "AU45_blink")

# --- Plot REAL Basis Matrix ---
rownames(model_nmf_real$w) <- au_names
colnames(model_nmf_real$w) <- c("nmf1", "nmf2", "nmf3")

NMF::aheatmap(
  apply(model_nmf_real$w, 2, fn_maxnormalise), 
  main = "Basis Matrix (W) - REAL Videos (Normalised)", 
  color = colorRampPalette(brewer.pal(6, "Blues"))(100), 
  scale = "none", 
  Rowv = NA, Colv = NA
)

# Print the normalised matrix for REAL
print("--- REAL Videos: Normalised Basis Matrix (W) ---")
print(apply(model_nmf_real$w, 2, fn_maxnormalise))


# --- Plot FAKE Basis Matrix ---
rownames(model_nmf_fake$w) <- au_names
colnames(model_nmf_fake$w) <- c("nmf1", "nmf2", "nmf3")

NMF::aheatmap(
  apply(model_nmf_fake$w, 2, fn_maxnormalise), 
  main = "Basis Matrix (W) - FAKE Videos (Normalised)", 
  color = colorRampPalette(brewer.pal(6, "Blues"))(100), 
  scale = "none", 
  Rowv = NA, Colv = NA
)

# Print the normalised matrix for FAKE
print("--- FAKE Videos: Normalised Basis Matrix (W) ---")
print(apply(model_nmf_fake$w, 2, fn_maxnormalise))

```

# Validation

## Reconstruction

```{r}
V_reconstructed <- model_nmf$w %*% diag(model_nmf$d) %*% model_nmf$h

V_original <- input_matrix

nmf_error <- V_original - V_reconstructed

# Calculate reconstruction error metrics
nmf_rmse <- sqrt(mean(nmf_error^2))
nmf_nrmse <- nmf_rmse / (max(V_original) - min(V_original))
nmf_mae <- mean(abs(nmf_error))
nmf_r2 <- 1 - sum(nmf_error^2) / sum((V_original - mean(V_original))^2)

dta_nmf_recon_err <- data.frame(
  Metric = c("RMSE", "NRMSE", "MAE", "R2"),
  Value = c(nmf_rmse, nmf_nrmse, nmf_mae, nmf_r2)
)

dta_nmf_recon_err
```

RMSE = 0.8818

NRMSE = 0.0646

MAE = 0.6503

R2 = 0.4415

After normalisation fix:

RMSE = 0.7868

NRMSE = 0.0436

MAE = 0.5294

R2 = 0.4167

The reconstruction error metrics are, on the whole, better now that the normalisation fix has been implemented. The R2 is slightly worse because the total variance is now much greater (as variance between videos is now preserved), so the R2 is always going to be worse.

## Visualisation of Reconstruction Quality

```{r}
target_au_plot <- "AU07_lid_tightener"
target_video_plot <- unique(dta_nmf_input_train$video_filename)[1]

bind_rows(
  as.data.frame(t(V_original)) %>%
    mutate(
      frame = dta_nmf_input_train$frame,
      video_filename = dta_nmf_input_train$video_filename,
      source = "Original"
    ) %>%
    pivot_longer(
      cols = all_of(rownames(model_nmf$w)),
      names_to = "AU",
      values_to = "activation"
    ),
  
  as.data.frame(t(V_reconstructed)) %>%
    mutate(
      frame = dta_nmf_input_train$frame,
      video_filename = dta_nmf_input_train$video_filename,
      source = "Reconstructed"
    ) %>%
    pivot_longer(
      cols = all_of(rownames(model_nmf$w)),
      names_to = "AU",
      values_to = "activation"
    )
) %>%
  filter(video_filename == target_video_plot, AU == target_au_plot) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = frame, y = activation, colour = source)) +
  geom_line(size = 1)
```

# CMFTS Features

## Install CMFTS Package

```{r}
# Install dependencies
#devtools::install_github("cbergmeir/tsExpKit")

# Install CMFTS package (uncomment if first run)
#devtools::install_github("fjbaldan/CMFTS")
```

## Perform CMFTS Feature Extraction

```{r}
dta_cmfts_input <- dta_nmf_output %>%
  select(video_filename, isfakeorreal, emotion, emotion_type, valence, frame, nmf1, nmf2, nmf3) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c("nmf1", "nmf2", "nmf3"),
               names_to = "component",
               values_to = "activation") %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = frame,
              values_from = activation,
              names_prefix = "fr_")

frame_cols <- grep("^fr_", names(dta_cmfts_input), value = TRUE)

head(dta_cmfts_input)
```

Function for calculating CMFTS features:

```{r}
fn_extract_cmfts_features <- function(ts_values) {
  ts_matrix <- matrix(as.numeric(ts_values), nrow = 1)
  cmfts_features <- cmfts::cmfts(dataset = ts_matrix)
  return(cmfts_features)
}
```

```{r}
# Apply feature extraction row-wise over the frame columns
dta_cmfts_output <- apply(
  dta_cmfts_input[, frame_cols], 
  1, 
  fn_extract_cmfts_features
  ) %>%
  do.call(rbind, .) %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  cbind(
    dta_cmfts_input[, c("video_filename", "isfakeorreal", "emotion", "emotion_type", "valence", "component")],
    .
  )

head(dta_cmfts_output)
```

## CMFTS Pre-Processing

First step is to concatenate the data so that each video has one row. This will result in each CMFTS feature having three columns (one for each NMF component). This is to make the data a suitable format for classifiers. More on this in `full_dataset_NMF_analysis.Rmd`.

```{r}
dta_cmfts_output_concat <- dta_cmfts_output %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = component,
    values_from = -c(video_filename, component),
    names_glue = "{.value}_{component}"
  )

# Remove duplicate columns
dta_cmfts_output_concat <- dta_cmfts_output_concat[, -3:-6]
dta_cmfts_output_concat <- dta_cmfts_output_concat[, -5:-8]
# Rename isfakeorreal & emotion columns
colnames(dta_cmfts_output_concat)[2] <- c("isfakeorreal")
colnames(dta_cmfts_output_concat)[3] <- c("emotion")
colnames(dta_cmfts_output_concat)[4] <- c("emotion_type")
colnames(dta_cmfts_output_concat)[5] <- c("valence")

head(dta_cmfts_output_concat)
```

### Missing Values

```{r}
dta_cmfts_output_concat %>%
  summarise(across(everything(), ~ sum(is.na(.)))) %>%
  pivot_longer(everything(),
               names_to = "column",
               values_to = "n_missing") %>%
  filter(n_missing > 0)
```

We can remove these columns.

```{r}
# Drop permutation entropy columns
dta_cmfts_output_concat <- dta_cmfts_output_concat[, -15:-17]
```

### Infinite Values

```{r}
dta_cmfts_output_concat %>%
  summarise(across(
    .cols = where(is.numeric),
    .fns  = ~ sum(is.infinite(.))
  )) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols       = everything(),
    names_to   = "column",
    values_to  = "n_infinite"
  )
```

Only Shannon entropy columns have infinite values. We can impute with `missForest` like in the previous Rmd script.

```{r}
# Convert Infs to NA
for(col in names(dta_cmfts_output_concat)) {
  dta_cmfts_output_concat[[col]][is.infinite(dta_cmfts_output_concat[[col]]) ] <- NA
}

set.seed(12345)

# Copy dta_cmfts_output_concat
dta_cmfts_output_imputed <- dta_cmfts_output_concat

# Get all numeric columns into a matrix
tmp <- as.matrix(dta_cmfts_output_imputed[, sapply(dta_cmfts_output_imputed, is.numeric)])
tmp[!is.finite(tmp)] <- NA

# Run missForest
impute_res <- missForest::missForest(tmp,
                         ntree = 100,
                         maxiter = 10,
                         verbose = TRUE)

dta_cmfts_output_imputed[, colnames(tmp)] <- impute_res$ximp

impute_res$OOBerror
```

NRMSE: 0.0011

After normalisation fix: NRMSE = 0.0036.

### Features with Zero SD

Features with SD = 0 contribute no meaningful information and can be removed.

```{r}
sds_zerosd <- sapply(dta_cmfts_output_imputed, function(x) {
  if(is.numeric(x)) sd(x, na.rm=T) else NA
})

# List features that do not meet the threshold
names(sds_zerosd)[is.na(sds_zerosd) | (sds_zerosd == 0)]

# Drop features with zero SD
dta_cmfts_output_imputed_zerosd <- dta_cmfts_output_imputed[, names(sds_zerosd)[is.na(sds_zerosd) | (sds_zerosd > 0)]]
```

This has removed 9 features.

# Feature Selection

## Correlation

Starting with a quick correlation-based elimination is a good way to find out which columns can be removed to reduce pairwise correlations. Columns that are highly correlated with one another are likely to be redundant for the classifier.

```{r}
# Cutoff 0.9
cat("ZeroSD\n\nCutoff = 0.9\n")
caret::findCorrelation(cor(dta_cmfts_output_imputed_zerosd[, 6:116]), cutoff = 0.9)

# Cutoff 0.95
cat("\nCutoff = 0.95\n")
caret::findCorrelation(cor(dta_cmfts_output_imputed_zerosd[, 6:116]), cutoff = 0.95)
```

A cutoff of 0.9 flagged 9 features for removal due to being highly correlated with at least one other feature. A cutoff point of 0.95 flagged no features for removal, same as before.

After normalisation fix:

A cutoff of 0.9 flagged 12 features for removal and a cutoff of 0.95 flagged 1 feature for removal.

## Recursive Feature Elimination (RFE)

```{r}
x_train_zerosd <- dta_cmfts_output_imputed_zerosd[, 6:116]
# y_train_zerosd <- as.factor(dta_cmfts_output_imputed_zerosd$isfakeorreal)
y_train_zerosd <- as.factor(dta_cmfts_output_imputed_zerosd$valence)

# Set up RFE control using random forest (fitting params)
control <- caret::rfeControl(functions = caret::rfFuncs,
                      method = "cv",
                      number = 10,
                      verbose = TRUE)  # 10-fold cross validation

# Define subset sizes to evaluate
# Model will start with 5 predictors, incrementing by 5
subsetSizes <- seq(5, ncol(x_train_zerosd), by = 5)

# RFE
set.seed(12345)
rfeResults_zerosd <- caret::rfe(x = x_train_zerosd,
                     y = y_train_zerosd,
                     sizes = subsetSizes,
                     rfeControl = control)

# Results
print(rfeResults_zerosd)
plot(rfeResults_zerosd)
```

After pair preservation:

Top 5 features:

1.  diff2_acf1_nmf1
2.  diff1_acf1_nmf1
3.  spike_nmf1
4.  sample_entropy_nmf1
5.  stability_nmf3

RFE identified an optimal subset of 55 features (accuracy = 52.72%).

After normalisation fix:

RFE identified an optimal subset of 40 features (accuracy = 45.68%, kappa = -0.09).

Top 5 features:

1.  nforbiden_nmf1
2.  e_acf10_nmf2
3.  diff2_acf1_nmf2
4.  diff1_acf10_nmf2
5.  diff2x_pacf5_nmf1

Predicting for valence:

RFE identified an optimal subset of 111 features (Accuracy = 76.03%, Kappa = 0.6125).

Top 5 features:

1.  shannon_entropy_CS_nmf1
2.  shannon_entropy_SG_nmf2
3.  shannon_entropy_CS_nmf3
4.  shannon_entropy_SG_nmf3
5.  linearity_nmf3

## Boruta

```{r, fig.height=8, fig.width=10}
set.seed(12345)

# Create a subset of data including only the outcome and predictors
# boruta_train_zsd <- dta_cmfts_output_imputed_zerosd[, c("isfakeorreal", 
#                                                         names(dta_cmfts_output_imputed_zerosd)[6:116])]

boruta_train_zsd <- dta_cmfts_output_imputed_zerosd[, c("valence", 
                                                        names(dta_cmfts_output_imputed_zerosd)[6:116])]

# Make sure outcome variable is factor
# boruta_train_zsd$isfakeorreal <- as.factor(boruta_train_zsd$isfakeorreal)
boruta_train_zsd$valence <- as.factor(boruta_train_zsd$valence)

# Boruta
# boruta_output_zsd <- Boruta::Boruta(isfakeorreal ~ ., data = boruta_train_zsd, doTrace = 2)
boruta_output_zsd <- Boruta::Boruta(valence ~ ., data = boruta_train_zsd, doTrace = 2)

# Output
# print(boruta_output_zsd)
# plot(boruta_output_zsd, las = 2)

# Solve tentative features
Boruta::TentativeRoughFix(boruta_output_zsd)

# Output
print(boruta_output_zsd)
par(mar = c(12, 4, 4, 2) + 0.1)
plot(boruta_output_zsd, las = 2)

# Solve tentative features
Boruta::TentativeRoughFix(boruta_output_zsd)

# get only important features
print(Boruta::getSelectedAttributes(boruta_output_zsd, withTentative = F))

# ranked
# 1. Get statistics for all attributes, including their median importance
feature_stats <- Boruta::attStats(boruta_output_zsd)

# 2. Add the feature names as a column to the data frame
feature_stats$features <- rownames(feature_stats)

# 3. Filter for only "Confirmed" features and sort by importance (descending)
ranked_features <- feature_stats %>%
  #dplyr::filter(decision == "Confirmed") %>%
  dplyr::arrange(desc(medianImp))

# Print the ranked table
print(ranked_features)

# To get just a vector of the ranked feature names:
ranked_feature_names <- ranked_features$features
print(ranked_feature_names)
```

Boruta only finds 2 important features this time:

1.  diff1_acf1_nmf1
2.  diff1x_pacf5_nmf3

Both of these were present in previous Boruta outputs.

Update after pair preservation:

Boruta only finds 1 important feature:

1.  diff2_acf1_nmf1

After normalisation fix:

Boruta found no important features (1 tentative initially).

The top 5 most important features:

1.  aproximation_entropy_nmf2 (tentative initially)
2.  nforbiden_nmf1
3.  nforbiden_nmf2
4.  diff1x_pacf5_nm2
5.  diff2_acf1_nmf2

I will use the top 5 RFE features first.

Predicting valence:

Boruta initially found 42 important features.

Top 5:

1.  shannon_entropy_CS_nmf1
2.  shannon_entropyCS_nmf3
3.  shannon_entropy_SG_nmf2
4.  shannon_entropy_SG_nmf3
5.  linearity_nmf3

Exact same as top 5 RFE features.

# Create Test Set

To create the test set, we need to project the NMF model onto the AU data from the test set, then derive CMFTS features from these.

## Project onto Test Set

```{r}
library(Matrix)
library(nnls)

# 1. Extract scaled basis matrix from model
project_W_train_raw <- model_nmf$w
project_d_train <- as.numeric(model_nmf$d)
project_W_train <- as.matrix(project_W_train_raw %*% Diagonal(x = project_d_train))

# 2. Set up loop to project each video

# Empty list to store final activation matrices
project_activations_list <- list()

for (video_name in unique(dta_nmf_input_test$video_filename)) {
  # Prepare matrix for current video
  project_V_new <- dta_nmf_input_test %>%
    filter(video_filename == video_name) %>%
    select(starts_with("AU")) %>%
    as.matrix()
  
  # Transpose so that features are rows and frames are columns
  project_v_new <- t(project_V_new)
  
  # Set up variables for inner NNLS loop
  project_k <- ncol(project_W_train)
  project_n_new <- ncol(project_v_new)
  project_h_new <- matrix(NA_real_, nrow = project_k, ncol = project_n_new)
  
  # Project each frame using NNLS
  for (i in seq_len(project_n_new)) {
    project_h_new[, i] <- nnls(project_W_train, project_v_new[, i])$x
  }
  
  # Apply scaling to projected H matrix
  project_h_new_scaled <- sweep(project_h_new, 1, project_d_train, "*")
  
  # Transpose final activation matrix so its frames x components
  project_h_new_transposed <- t(project_h_new_scaled)
  
  # Store result in list
  project_activations_list[[video_name]] <- project_h_new_transposed
}

cat("Projection complete.\n")
cat(paste("The list now contains", length(project_activations_list), "activation matrices.\n\n"))
cat("Dimensions of activation matrix for first video (frames x components):\n")
print(dim(project_activations_list[[1]]))
```

## Validate

```{r}
project_V_orig <- dta_nmf_input_test %>%
  #filter(video_filename == names(project_activations_list)[1]) %>%
  select(starts_with("AU")) %>%
  as.matrix()

project_H_new <- do.call(rbind, project_activations_list)

# Reconstruct
project_V_recon <- project_H_new %*% t(model_nmf$w)

# Calculate R squared
project_r2_validation <- 1 - sum((project_V_orig - project_V_recon)^2) / sum((project_V_orig - mean(project_V_orig))^2)

cat(sprintf("Reconstruction R2 for entire test set: %.4f\n", project_r2_validation))
```

The reconstruction value of 0.4383 means that the trained NMF model can explain approximately 44% of the total variance in the unseen test set. This value is the best R2 so far, across all previous analyses.

Although not perfect, facial AU data is very complex, so 0.4383 seems a reasonable result, indicating that the model has successfully captured meaningful patterns in the data.

After normalisation fix:

R2 = 0.4452

## Create Final Test Set

```{r}
dta_h_matrix_test <- as.data.frame(project_H_new)
colnames(dta_h_matrix_test) <- paste0("nmf", 1:ncol(dta_h_matrix_test))

dta_metadata_test <- dta_nmf_input_test %>%
  select(video_filename, corresponding_video, scene_name, isfakeorreal, emotion, emotion_type, valence, frame)

dta_nmf_test_output <- bind_cols(dta_metadata_test, dta_h_matrix_test)

head(dta_nmf_test_output)
```

## Calculate CMFTS Features

The next step is to take the projected NMF values and calculate CMFTS features in the same way as before.

```{r}
dta_cmfts_test_input <- dta_nmf_test_output %>%
  select(video_filename, isfakeorreal, emotion, emotion_type, valence, frame, nmf1, nmf2, nmf3) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c("nmf1", "nmf2", "nmf3"),
               names_to = "component",
               values_to = "activation") %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = frame,
              values_from = activation,
              names_prefix = "fr_")

frame_cols <- grep("^fr_", names(dta_cmfts_test_input), value = TRUE)

head(dta_cmfts_test_input)
```

```{r}
# Apply feature extraction row-wise over the frame columns
dta_cmfts_test_output <- apply(
  dta_cmfts_test_input[, frame_cols], 
  1, 
  fn_extract_cmfts_features
  ) %>%
  do.call(rbind, .) %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  cbind(
    dta_cmfts_test_input[, c("video_filename", "isfakeorreal", "emotion", "emotion_type", "valence", "component")],
    .
  )

head(dta_cmfts_test_output)
```

Now time to preprocess in the same way as before.

## CMFTS Preprocessing

```{r}
dta_cmfts_test_output_concat <- dta_cmfts_test_output %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = component,
    values_from = -c(video_filename, component),
    names_glue = "{.value}_{component}"
  )

# Remove duplicate columns
dta_cmfts_test_output_concat <- dta_cmfts_test_output_concat[, -3:-6]
dta_cmfts_test_output_concat <- dta_cmfts_test_output_concat[, -5:-8]
# Rename isfakeorreal & emotion columns
colnames(dta_cmfts_test_output_concat)[2] <- c("isfakeorreal")
colnames(dta_cmfts_test_output_concat)[3] <- c("emotion")
colnames(dta_cmfts_test_output_concat)[4] <- c("emotion_type")
colnames(dta_cmfts_test_output_concat)[5] <- c("valence")

head(dta_cmfts_test_output_concat)
```

Missing values:

```{r}
dta_cmfts_test_output_concat %>%
  summarise(across(everything(), ~ sum(is.na(.)))) %>%
  pivot_longer(everything(),
               names_to = "column",
               values_to = "n_missing") %>%
  filter(n_missing > 0)
```

```{r}
# Drop permutation entropy columns
dta_cmfts_test_output_concat <- dta_cmfts_test_output_concat[, -15:-17]
```

Infinite values:

```{r}
dta_cmfts_test_output_concat %>%
  summarise(across(
    .cols = where(is.numeric),
    .fns  = ~ sum(is.infinite(.))
  )) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols       = everything(),
    names_to   = "column",
    values_to  = "n_infinite"
  )
```

```{r}
# Convert Infs to NA
for(col in names(dta_cmfts_test_output_concat)) {
  dta_cmfts_test_output_concat[[col]][is.infinite(dta_cmfts_test_output_concat[[col]]) ] <- NA
}

set.seed(12345)

# Copy dta_cmfts_output_concat
dta_cmfts_test_output_imputed <- dta_cmfts_test_output_concat

# Get all numeric columns into a matrix
tmp <- as.matrix(dta_cmfts_test_output_imputed[, sapply(dta_cmfts_test_output_imputed, is.numeric)])
tmp[!is.finite(tmp)] <- NA

# Run missForest
impute_res <- missForest::missForest(tmp,
                         ntree = 100,
                         maxiter = 10,
                         verbose = TRUE)

dta_cmfts_test_output_imputed[, colnames(tmp)] <- impute_res$ximp

impute_res$OOBerror
```

NRMSE = 0.0009

Very low reconstruction error indicates it worked properly.

After normalisation fix: NRMSE = 0.0039.

Features with Zero SD:

```{r}
sds_zerosd <- sapply(dta_cmfts_test_output_imputed, function(x) {
  if(is.numeric(x)) sd(x, na.rm=T) else NA
})

# List features that do not meet the threshold
names(sds_zerosd)[is.na(sds_zerosd) | (sds_zerosd == 0)]

# Drop features with zero SD
dta_cmfts_test_output_imputed_zerosd <- dta_cmfts_test_output_imputed[, names(sds_zerosd)[is.na(sds_zerosd) | (sds_zerosd > 0)]]
```

The test set is ready: `dta_cmfts_test_output_imputed_zerosd`.

# Imbalanced Emotion Class

As we saw earlier, there is an imbalance in the emotion class (222 emotion, 148 no emotion). Now that we have the CMFTS features, we can apply SMOTE upsamping. SMOTE is better suited to the CMFTS data as it is not high dimensional or a time series, unlike the AU data before NMF.

```{r}
# # Make sure emotion column is factor
# dta_cmfts_output_imputed_zerosd$emotion <- as.factor(
#   dta_cmfts_output_imputed_zerosd$emotion
# )
# 
# # Show class imbalance before
# cat("Before SMOTE:\n")
# print(table(dta_cmfts_output_imputed_zerosd$emotion))
# 
# # SMOTE recipe
# smote_recipe <- recipes::recipe(emotion ~ ., data = dta_cmfts_output_imputed_zerosd) %>%
#   recipes::update_role(video_filename, isfakeorreal, new_role = "ID") %>%
#   themis::step_smote(emotion, over_ratio = 1) %>%  # over_ratio = 1 upsampling minority
#   recipes::prep()
# 
# dta_cmfts_emotion_balanced <- recipes::bake(smote_recipe, new_data = NULL)
# 
# # Class balance after
# cat("\nAfter SMOTE:\n")
# print(table(dta_cmfts_emotion_balanced$emotion))
```

The final balanced training set is `dta_cmfts_emotion_balanced`.

UPDATE:

This cannot be used for model training as the new rows do not have associated real/fake labels. Instead, we can oversample using bootstrapping.

```{r}
set.seed(12345)

# Separate the minority and majority
majority_data <- dta_cmfts_output_imputed_zerosd %>%
  filter(emotion == "yes")

minority_data <- dta_cmfts_output_imputed_zerosd %>%
  filter(emotion == "no")

# Bootstrapping on minority class
minority_data_oversampled <- minority_data %>%
  sample_n(size = nrow(majority_data), replace = TRUE)

# Combine to create balanced dta
dta_cmfts_emotion_balanced <- bind_rows(majority_data, minority_data_oversampled)

# Verify
cat("Before:\n")
print(table(dta_cmfts_output_imputed_zerosd$emotion))

cat("\nAfter:\n")
print(table(dta_cmfts_emotion_balanced$emotion))
```

Balancing valence:

-   Balancing the emotion_type class will be too extreme since the minority class has 6 and the majority class has 148.

```{r}
set.seed(12345)

target_size <- nrow(dta_cmfts_output_imputed_zerosd %>% filter(valence == "neutral"))

# Separate data into the three classes
neutral_data <- dta_cmfts_output_imputed_zerosd %>% filter(valence == "neutral")
positive_data <- dta_cmfts_output_imputed_zerosd %>% filter(valence == "positive")
negative_data <- dta_cmfts_output_imputed_zerosd %>% filter(valence == "negative")

# Oversample minority classes to match target size
positive_data <- positive_data %>%
  sample_n(size = target_size, replace = T)
negative_data <- negative_data %>%
  sample_n(size = target_size, replace = T)

# Combine into new df
dta_cmfts_valenced_balanced <- bind_rows(neutral_data, positive_data, negative_data)

# Check
cat("Before:\n")
print(table(dta_cmfts_output_imputed_zerosd$valence))
cat("After:\n")
print(table(dta_cmfts_valenced_balanced$valence))

```

# Classifiers

I will create two models for each classifier.

-   Model 1 = Only features confirmed as important by Boruta (n=2)

-   Model 2 = Same feature types but across all components (n=6)

The reason for this stems from the idea that the three NMF components represent interrelated aspects of facial expression, so if a feature type was most discriminative for one component, its behaviour in other components might still hold relevant information for classification.

```{r}
set.seed(12345)

# Train set (UNCOMMENT FOR IMBALANCED EMOTION)
# dta_cmfts_output_imputed_zerosd$isfakeorreal <- factor(
#   dta_cmfts_output_imputed_zerosd$isfakeorreal,
#   levels = c("real", "fake")
# )

dta_cmfts_emotion_balanced$isfakeorreal <- factor(
  dta_cmfts_emotion_balanced$isfakeorreal,
  levels = c("real", "fake")
)

# dta_cmfts_valenced_balanced$valence <- factor(
#   dta_cmfts_valenced_balanced$valence,
#   levels = c("positive", "neutral", "negative")
# )

# UNCOMMENT FOR REAL/FAKE PREDICTION
boruta_selected_features <- c(
  "nforbiden_nmf1",
  "e_acf10_nmf2",
  "diff2_acf1_nmf2",
  "diff1_acf10_nmf2",
  "diff2x_pacf5_nmf1"
)

# Valence
# boruta_selected_features <- c(
#   "shannon_entropy_CS_nmf1",
#   "shannon_entropy_CS_nmf3",
#   "shannon_entropy_SG_nmf2",
#   "shannon_entropy_SG_nmf3",
#   "linearity_nmf3"
# )

# Select these features plus the outcome variable
# dta_classifier_train_new <- dta_cmfts_output_imputed_zerosd %>%
#   select(video_filename, emotion, isfakeorreal, all_of(boruta_selected_features))

# UNCOMMENT FOR REAL FAKE
dta_classifier_train_new <- dta_cmfts_emotion_balanced %>%
  select(video_filename, emotion, isfakeorreal, all_of(boruta_selected_features))

# Valence
# dta_classifier_train_new <- dta_cmfts_valenced_balanced %>%
#   select(video_filename, isfakeorreal, emotion_type, valence, all_of(boruta_selected_features))

# UNCOMMENT FOR REAL FAKE
base_feature_types <- c(
  "nforbiden",
  "e_acf10",
  "diff2_acf1",
  "diff1_acf10",
  "diff2x_pacf5"
  )

# Valence
# base_feature_types <- c(
#   "shannon_entropy_CS",
#   "shannon_entropy_SG",
#   "linearity"
#   )

all_component_features <- character()
for (base_feat in base_feature_types) {
  for (comp_num in 1:3) {
    all_component_features <- c(all_component_features, paste0(base_feat, "_nmf", comp_num))
  }
}

# Unbalanced emotion
# dta_classifier_train_new_all <- dta_cmfts_output_imputed_zerosd %>%
#   select(video_filename, emotion, isfakeorreal, all_of(all_component_features))

# UNCOMMENT FOR REAL FAKE
dta_classifier_train_new_all <- dta_cmfts_emotion_balanced %>%
  select(video_filename, emotion, isfakeorreal, all_of(all_component_features))

# Valence
# dta_classifier_train_new_all <- dta_cmfts_valenced_balanced %>%
#   select(video_filename, isfakeorreal, emotion_type, valence, all_of(all_component_features))

# dta_classifier_train_new$video_filename <- dta_cmfts_output_imputed_zerosd$video_filename

dta_classifier_train_new$video_filename <- dta_cmfts_emotion_balanced$video_filename

# dta_classifier_train_new$video_filename <- dta_cmfts_valenced_balanced$video_filename



# Test set
dta_cmfts_test_output_imputed_zerosd$isfakeorreal <- factor(
  dta_cmfts_test_output_imputed_zerosd$isfakeorreal,
  levels = c("real", "fake")
)

dta_classifier_test_new <- dta_cmfts_test_output_imputed_zerosd %>%
  select(video_filename, emotion, isfakeorreal, all_of(boruta_selected_features))

dta_classifier_test_new_all <- dta_cmfts_test_output_imputed_zerosd %>%
  select(video_filename, emotion, isfakeorreal, all_of(all_component_features))

dta_classifier_test_new$video_filename <- dta_cmfts_test_output_imputed_zerosd$video_filename

# Valence
# dta_cmfts_test_output_imputed_zerosd$valence <- factor(
#   dta_cmfts_test_output_imputed_zerosd$valence,
#   levels = c("positive", "neutral", "negative")
# )
# 
# dta_classifier_test_new <- dta_cmfts_test_output_imputed_zerosd %>%
#   select(video_filename, isfakeorreal, emotion_type, valence, all_of(boruta_selected_features))
# 
# dta_classifier_test_new_all <- dta_cmfts_test_output_imputed_zerosd %>%
#   select(video_filename, isfakeorreal, emotion_type, valence, all_of(all_component_features))
# 
# dta_classifier_test_new$video_filename <- dta_cmfts_test_output_imputed_zerosd$video_filename
```

## Random Forest

### Model 1 (Boruta only)

```{r}
library(MLmetrics)
# Cross validation setup
classifier_control <- caret::trainControl(
  method = "none",
  classProbs = TRUE,
  summaryFunction = caret::multiClassSummary
)

set.seed(12345)

# rf_fit_model1 <- caret::train(
#   valence ~ ., # nmf
#   data = dta_classifier_train_new[, -(1:3)],
#   method = "rf",
#   metric = "ROC",
#   trControl = classifier_control,
#   tuneGrid = expand.grid(mtry = 4)
# )

rf_fit_model1 <- caret::train(
  isfakeorreal ~ ., # nmf
  data = dta_classifier_train_new[, -(1:2)],
  method = "rf",
  metric = "ROC",
  trControl = classifier_control,
  tuneGrid = expand.grid(mtry = 4)
)


print(rf_fit_model1)
plot(caret::varImp(rf_fit_model1)) # To see feature importance

# print(rf_fit_model_emotion)
```

Evaluate on unseen data:

```{r}
rf_model_1_performance <- caret::confusionMatrix(
  data = predict(rf_fit_model1, newdata = dta_classifier_test_new),
  reference = dta_classifier_test_new$isfakeorreal,
  positive = "fake"
)

# Valence
# rf_model_1_performance <- caret::confusionMatrix(
#   data = predict(rf_fit_model1, newdata = dta_classifier_test_new),
#   reference = dta_classifier_test_new$valence
# )

print(rf_model_1_performance)

# # Emotion
# rf_model_1_emotion_performance <- caret::confusionMatrix(
#   data = predict(rf_fit_model1_emotion, newdata = dta_classifier_test_new),
#   reference = dta_classifier_test_new$emotion,
#   positive = "yes"
# )
# 
# print(rf_model_1_emotion_performance)

```

Before pair preservation:

Accuracy = 46.24%

95% CI = (0.3584, 0.5688)

NIR = 0.5699

P = 0.9857

Kappa = -0.0578

Sensitivity = 52.50%

Specificity = 41.51%

After:

Accuracy = 65.96%

95% CI = (0.5546, 0.7542)

NIR = 0.5

P = 0.001294 (P \< 0.01)

Kappa = 0.3191

Sensitivity = 72.34%

Specificity = 59.57%

After oversampling emotion:

Accuracy = 62.77%

95% CI = (0.5218, 0.7252)

NIR = 0.5

P = 0.008605 (P \< 0.01)

Kappa = 0.2553

Sensitivity = 68.09%

Specificity = 57.45%

### Model 2 (All Features)

```{r}
# Cross validation setup
classifier_control <- caret::trainControl(
  method = "none",
  classProbs = TRUE,
  summaryFunction = caret::twoClassSummary
)

set.seed(12345)

rf_fit_model2 <- caret::train(
  isfakeorreal ~ ., # nmf
  data = dta_classifier_train_new_all[, -(1:2)],
  method = "rf",
  metric = "ROC",
  trControl = classifier_control,
  tuneGrid = expand.grid(mtry = 4)
)

# Valence
# rf_fit_model2 <- caret::train(
#   valence ~ ., # nmf
#   data = dta_classifier_train_new_all[, -(1:3)],
#   method = "rf",
#   metric = "ROC",
#   trControl = classifier_control,
#   tuneGrid = expand.grid(mtry = 4)
# )

#Emotion
# rf_fit_model2_emotion <- caret::train(
#   emotion ~ ., # nmf
#   data = dta_classifier_train_new_all[, c("emotion", "diff2_acf1_nmf1", "diff2_acf1_nmf2", "diff2_acf1_nmf3")],
#   method = "rf",
#   metric = "ROC",
#   trControl = classifier_control,
#   tuneGrid = expand.grid(mtry = 1)
# )

print(rf_fit_model2)
plot(caret::varImp(rf_fit_model2)) # To see feature importance
```

Evaluate on unseen data:

```{r}
rf_model_2_performance <- caret::confusionMatrix(
  data = predict(rf_fit_model2, newdata = dta_classifier_test_new_all),
  reference = dta_classifier_test_new_all$isfakeorreal,
  positive = "fake"
)

# Valence
# rf_model_2_performance <- caret::confusionMatrix(
#   data = predict(rf_fit_model2, newdata = dta_classifier_test_new_all),
#   reference = dta_classifier_test_new_all$valence
# )

print(rf_model_2_performance)


# rf_model_2_emotion_performance <- caret::confusionMatrix(
#   data = predict(rf_fit_model2_emotion, newdata = dta_classifier_test_new_all),
#   reference = dta_classifier_test_new_all$emotion,
#   positive = "yes"
# )
# 
# print(rf_model_2_emotion_performance)


# SAVE RDS
saveRDS(rf_fit_model2, "models/rf_fit_model2.RDS")
```

After:

Accuracy = 57.45%

95% CI = (0.4682, 0.6759)

NIR = 0.5

P = 0.08983

Kappa = 0.1489

Sensitivity = 63.83%

Specificity = 51.06%

After oversampling:

Accuracy = 58.51%

95% CI = (0.4788, 0.6859)

NIR = 0.5

P = 0.06069

Kappa = 0.1702

Sensitivity = 0.6383

Specificity = 0.5319

## C5.0B

### Model 1 (Boruta Selected Features)

```{r}
set.seed(12345)

# c50_fit_model1 <- caret::train(
#   isfakeorreal ~ .,
#   data = dta_classifier_train_new[, -(1:2)],
#   method = "C5.0",
#   metric = "ROC",
#   trControl = classifier_control,
#   tuneGrid = expand.grid(
#     model = "tree",
#     trials = 20,  # number of boosting iterations
#     winnow = FALSE
#   )
# )

# Valence
c50_fit_model1 <- caret::train(
  valence ~ .,
  data = dta_classifier_train_new[, -(1:3)],
  method = "C5.0",
  metric = "ROC",
  trControl = classifier_control,
  tuneGrid = expand.grid(
    model = "tree",
    trials = 20,  # number of boosting iterations
    winnow = FALSE
  )
)

# Emotion
# c50_fit_model1_emotion <- caret::train(
#   emotion ~ .,
#   data = dta_classifier_train_new[, c("emotion", "diff2_acf1_nmf1")],
#   method = "C5.0",
#   metric = "ROC",
#   trControl = classifier_control,
#   tuneGrid = expand.grid(
#     model = "tree",
#     trials = 20,  # number of boosting iterations
#     winnow = FALSE
#   )
# )

print(c50_fit_model1)
plot(varImp(c50_fit_model1)) # To see feature importanc
```

Evaluate on unseen data:

```{r}
# c50_model_1_performance <- caret::confusionMatrix(
#   data = predict(c50_fit_model1, newdata = dta_classifier_test_new),
#   reference = dta_classifier_test_new$isfakeorreal,
#   positive = "fake"
# )

# Valence

c50_model_1_performance <- caret::confusionMatrix(
  data = predict(c50_fit_model1, newdata = dta_classifier_test_new),
  reference = dta_classifier_test_new$valence
)

print(c50_model_1_performance)

#Emotion
# c50_model_1_emotion_performance <- caret::confusionMatrix(
#   data = predict(c50_fit_model1_emotion, newdata = dta_classifier_test_new),
#   reference = dta_classifier_test_new$emotion,
#   positive = "yes"
# )
# 
# print(c50_model_1_emotion_performance)
```

Accuracy = 46.24%

95% CI = (0.3584, 0.5688)

NIR = 0.5699

P = 0.9857

Kappa = 0.0389

Sensitivity = 95.00%

Specificity = 9.43%

After:

Accuracy = 50%

NIR = 0.5

P = 0.541

Kappa = 0

Sensitivity = 0

Specificity = 1

It is now just classifying everything as real.

After oversampling:

Same.

### Model 2 (All Features)

```{r}
set.seed(12345)

# c50_fit_model2 <- caret::train(
#   isfakeorreal ~ .,
#   data = dta_classifier_train_new_all[, -(1:2)],
#   method = "C5.0",
#   metric = "ROC",
#   trControl = classifier_control,
#   tuneGrid = expand.grid(
#     model = "tree",
#     trials = 20,  # number of boosting iterations
#     winnow = FALSE
#   )
# )

# Valence
c50_fit_model2 <- caret::train(
  valence ~ .,
  data = dta_classifier_train_new_all[, -(1:3)],
  method = "C5.0",
  metric = "ROC",
  trControl = classifier_control,
  tuneGrid = expand.grid(
    model = "tree",
    trials = 20,  # number of boosting iterations
    winnow = FALSE
  )
)

# Emotion
# c50_fit_model2_emotion <- caret::train(
#   emotion ~ .,
#   data = dta_classifier_train_new_all[, c("emotion", "diff2_acf1_nmf1", "diff2_acf1_nmf2", "diff2_acf1_nmf3")],
#   method = "C5.0",
#   metric = "ROC",
#   trControl = classifier_control,
#   tuneGrid = expand.grid(
#     model = "tree",
#     trials = 20,  # number of boosting iterations
#     winnow = FALSE
#   )
# )

print(c50_fit_model2)
plot(varImp(c50_fit_model2)) # To see feature importanc
```

Evaluate on unseen data:

```{r}
# c50_model_2_performance <- caret::confusionMatrix(
#   data = predict(c50_fit_model2, newdata = dta_classifier_test_new_all),
#   reference = dta_classifier_test_new_all$isfakeorreal,
#   positive = "fake"
# )

# Valence
c50_model_2_performance <- caret::confusionMatrix(
  data = predict(c50_fit_model2, newdata = dta_classifier_test_new_all),
  reference = dta_classifier_test_new_all$valence
)

print(c50_model_2_performance)

# Emotion
# c50_model_2_emotion_performance <- caret::confusionMatrix(
#   data = predict(c50_fit_model2_emotion, newdata = dta_classifier_test_new_all),
#   reference = dta_classifier_test_new_all$emotion,
#   positive = "yes"
# )
# 
# print(c50_model_2_emotion_performance)
```

Same as model 1.

Possibly because in both models, C5.0B found that one of the features provided the best split, so the others were ignored. So both models are the same since they are only using one feature (the same feature).

After oversampling:

Accuracy = 61.7%

95% CI = (0.511, 0.7154)

NIR = 0.5

P = 0.01488 (P \< 0.05)

Kappa = 0.234

Sensitivity = 74.47%

Specificity = 48.94%

# Repeated Cross-Validation

The next step is to train the classifiers again but this time using repeated CV. We did not do this before due to the data leakage problem. However, we will do this now to compare results.

## Random Forest

### Model 1

```{r}
# Cross validation setup
classifier_control <- caret::trainControl(
  method = "repeatedcv",
  number = 5,
  repeats = 3,
  sampling = "smote",
  classProbs = TRUE,
  summaryFunction = caret::twoClassSummary
)

set.seed(12345)

rf_fit_model1_cv <- caret::train(
  isfakeorreal ~ ., # nmf
  data = dta_classifier_train_new[, 3:4],
  method = "rf",
  metric = "ROC",
  trControl = classifier_control,
  tuneLength = 5
)

print(rf_fit_model1_cv)
plot(caret::varImp(rf_fit_model1_cv)) # To see feature importance
```

Evaluate on unseen data:

```{r}
rf_model_1_cv_performance <- caret::confusionMatrix(
  data = predict(rf_fit_model1_cv, newdata = dta_classifier_test_new),
  reference = dta_classifier_test_new$isfakeorreal,
  positive = "fake"
)

print(rf_model_1_cv_performance)
```

Accuracy = 44.09%

95% CI = (0.338, 0.5476)

NIR = 0.5699

P = 0.99

Kappa = -0.1001

Sensitivity = 50.00%

Specificity = 39.62%

After:

Accuracy = 65.96%

### Model 2

```{r}
# Cross validation setup
classifier_control <- caret::trainControl(
  method = "repeatedcv",
  number = 5,
  repeats = 3,
  sampling = "smote",
  classProbs = TRUE,
  summaryFunction = caret::twoClassSummary
)

set.seed(12345)

rf_fit_model2_cv <- caret::train(
  isfakeorreal ~ ., # nmf
  data = dta_classifier_train_new_all[, 3:6],
  method = "rf",
  metric = "ROC",
  trControl = classifier_control,
  tuneLength = 5
)

print(rf_fit_model2_cv)
plot(caret::varImp(rf_fit_model2_cv)) # To see feature importance
```

Evaluate on unseen data:

```{r}
rf_model_2_cv_performance <- caret::confusionMatrix(
  data = predict(rf_fit_model2_cv, newdata = dta_classifier_test_new_all),
  reference = dta_classifier_test_new_all$isfakeorreal,
  positive = "fake"
)

print(rf_model_2_cv_performance)
```

Accuracy = 52.69%

95% CI = (0.4206, 0.6314)

NIR = 0.5699

P = 0.827

Kappa = 0.0854

Sensitivity = 67.50%

Specificity = 41.51%

After:

Accuracy = 58.51%

95% CI = (0.4788, 0.6859)

NIR = 0.5

P = 0.06069

Kappa = 0.33667

Sensitivity = 65.96%

Specificity = 51.06%

## C5.0B

### Model 1

```{r}
set.seed(12345)

c50_fit_model1_cv <- caret::train(
  isfakeorreal ~ .,
  data = dta_classifier_train_new[, 3:4],
  method = "C5.0",
  metric = "ROC",
  trControl = classifier_control,
  tuneGrid = expand.grid(
    model = "tree",
    trials = c(1, 10, 20),  # number of boosting iterations
    winnow = FALSE
  )
)

print(c50_fit_model1_cv)
plot(varImp(c50_fit_model1_cv)) # To see feature importanc
```

Evaluate on unseen data:

```{r}
c50_model_1_cv_performance <- caret::confusionMatrix(
  data = predict(c50_fit_model1_cv, newdata = dta_classifier_test_new),
  reference = dta_classifier_test_new$isfakeorreal,
  positive = "fake"
)

print(c50_model_1_cv_performance)
```

### Model 2

```{r}
set.seed(12345)

c50_fit_model2_cv <- caret::train(
  isfakeorreal ~ .,
  data = dta_classifier_train_new_all[, 3:9],
  method = "C5.0",
  metric = "ROC",
  trControl = classifier_control,
  tuneGrid = expand.grid(
    model = "tree",
    trials = c(1, 10, 20),  # number of boosting iterations
    winnow = FALSE
  )
)

print(c50_fit_model2_cv)
plot(varImp(c50_fit_model2_cv)) # To see feature importanc
```

Evaluate on unseen data:

```{r}
c50_model_2_cv_performance <- caret::confusionMatrix(
  data = predict(c50_fit_model2_cv, newdata = dta_classifier_test_new_all),
  reference = dta_classifier_test_new_all$isfakeorreal,
  positive = "fake"
)

print(c50_model_2_cv_performance)
```

C5.0B Model 1:

-   Accuracy = 46.24%

-   95% CI = (0.3584, 0.5688)

-   NIR = 0.5699

-   P = 0.9857

-   Kappa = 0.0389

-   Sensitivity = 95.00%

-   Specificity = 9.43%

C5.0B Model 2:

-   Accuracy = 46.24%

-   95% CI = (0.3584, 0.5688)

-   NIR = 0.5699

-   P = 0.9857

-   Kappa = 0.0389

-   Sensitivity = 95.00%

-   Specificity = 9.43%

Why are the C5.0B models still identical?

Cross-validation is used for hyperparameter tuning, not for building the final model. Since the same seed was used as before, and since the optimal parameters are the same, the resulting model is identical for the same reasons as before.

Why is RF slightly worse?

An example of model variance. Without CV, I chose a single hyperparameter (mtry = 4) and by chance, this produced a model that happened to be slightly better suited to the specific test set. With CV, it found the mtry value that performed best on average across the different fold subsets of the training data. This normally makes the model more robust and less likely to overfit, but happened to perform slightly worse on the test set. In theory, the version using CV is more methodologically sound and a more reliable way to build a generalisable model.

# 40 Held-Out Videos Evaluation

I will test all models on the 40 held-out videos from the human participant experiment as another test set.

But first, I will have to derive the CMFTS features for these videos by projecting the NMF model first.

## Project NMF to AU data

```{r}
dta_video_au_40 <- read.csv("../human_data_preprocess/dta_video_au_final.csv")

library(Matrix)
library(nnls)

# 1. Extract scaled basis matrix from model
project_W_train_raw <- model_nmf$w
project_d_train <- as.numeric(model_nmf$d)
project_W_train <- as.matrix(project_W_train_raw %*% Diagonal(x = project_d_train))

# 2. Set up loop to project each video

# Empty list to store final activation matrices
project_40_activations_list <- list()

for (video_name in unique(dta_video_au_40$stimnopath)) {
  # Prepare matrix for current video
  project_V_new <- dta_video_au_40 %>%
    filter(stimnopath == video_name) %>%
    select(starts_with("au")) %>%
    as.matrix()
  
  # Transpose so that features are rows and frames are columns
  project_v_new <- t(project_V_new)
  
  # Set up variables for inner NNLS loop
  project_k <- ncol(project_W_train)
  project_n_new <- ncol(project_v_new)
  project_h_new <- matrix(NA_real_, nrow = project_k, ncol = project_n_new)
  
  # Project each frame using NNLS
  for (i in seq_len(project_n_new)) {
    project_h_new[, i] <- nnls(project_W_train, project_v_new[, i])$x
  }
  
  # Apply scaling to projected H matrix
  project_h_new_scaled <- sweep(project_h_new, 1, project_d_train, "*")
  
  # Transpose final activation matrix so its frames x components
  project_h_new_transposed <- t(project_h_new_scaled)
  
  # Store result in list
  project_40_activations_list[[video_name]] <- project_h_new_transposed
}

cat("Projection complete.\n")
cat(paste("The list now contains", length(project_40_activations_list), "activation matrices.\n\n"))
cat("Dimensions of activation matrix for first video (frames x components):\n")
print(dim(project_40_activations_list[[1]]))
```

Validation:

```{r}
project_40_V_orig <- dta_video_au_40 %>%
  #filter(video_filename == names(project_activations_list)[1]) %>%
  select(starts_with("AU")) %>%
  as.matrix()

project_40_H_new <- do.call(rbind, project_40_activations_list)

# Reconstruct
project_40_V_recon <- project_40_H_new %*% t(model_nmf$w)

# Calculate R squared
project_40_r2_validation <- 1 - sum((project_40_V_orig - project_40_V_recon)^2) / sum((project_40_V_orig - mean(project_40_V_orig))^2)

cat(sprintf("Reconstruction R2 for entire test set: %.4f\n", project_40_r2_validation))
```

Reconstruction error for 40 held out video test set of ~~0.4096~~ 0.3441.

Create the final test set:

```{r}
dta_40_h_matrix_test <- as.data.frame(project_40_H_new)
colnames(dta_40_h_matrix_test) <- paste0("nmf", 1:ncol(dta_40_h_matrix_test))

dta_40_metadata_test <- dta_video_au_40 %>%
  select(stimnopath, isfakeorreal, emotion_yes_no_new, frame)

dta_40_nmf_test_output <- bind_cols(dta_40_metadata_test, dta_40_h_matrix_test)

head(dta_40_nmf_test_output)
```

## Derive CMFTS Features

```{r}
# Make sure max frames is 241
dta_40_nmf_test_output <- dta_40_nmf_test_output %>%
  filter(frame < 242)


dta_cmfts_40_test_input <- dta_40_nmf_test_output %>%
  select(stimnopath, isfakeorreal, emotion_yes_no_new, frame, nmf1, nmf2, nmf3) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c("nmf1", "nmf2", "nmf3"),
               names_to = "component",
               values_to = "activation") %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = frame,
              values_from = activation,
              names_prefix = "fr_")

frame_cols <- grep("^fr_", names(dta_cmfts_40_test_input), value = TRUE)

head(dta_cmfts_40_test_input)
```

```{r}
# Apply feature extraction row-wise over the frame columns
dta_cmfts_40_test_output <- apply(
  dta_cmfts_40_test_input[, frame_cols], 
  1, 
  fn_extract_cmfts_features
  ) %>%
  do.call(rbind, .) %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  cbind(
    dta_cmfts_40_test_input[, c("stimnopath", "isfakeorreal", "emotion_yes_no_new", "component")],
    .
  )

head(dta_cmfts_40_test_output)
```

## CMFTS Preprocessing

```{r}
dta_cmfts_40_test_output_concat <- dta_cmfts_40_test_output %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = component,
    values_from = -c(stimnopath, component),
    names_glue = "{.value}_{component}"
  )

# Remove duplicate columns
dta_cmfts_40_test_output_concat <- dta_cmfts_40_test_output_concat[, -3:-6]
# Rename isfakeorreal & emotion columns
colnames(dta_cmfts_40_test_output_concat)[2] <- c("isfakeorreal")
colnames(dta_cmfts_40_test_output_concat)[3] <- c("emotion")
colnames(dta_cmfts_40_test_output_concat)[1] <- c("video_filename")

head(dta_cmfts_40_test_output_concat)
```

Missing values:

```{r}
dta_cmfts_40_test_output_concat %>%
  summarise(across(everything(), ~ sum(is.na(.)))) %>%
  pivot_longer(everything(),
               names_to = "column",
               values_to = "n_missing") %>%
  filter(n_missing > 0)
```

```{r}
# Drop permutation entropy columns
dta_cmfts_40_test_output_concat <- dta_cmfts_40_test_output_concat[, -13:-15]
```

Infinite values:

```{r}
dta_cmfts_40_test_output_concat %>%
  summarise(across(
    .cols = where(is.numeric),
    .fns  = ~ sum(is.infinite(.))
  )) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols       = everything(),
    names_to   = "column",
    values_to  = "n_infinite"
  )
```

```{r}
# Convert Infs to NA
for(col in names(dta_cmfts_40_test_output_concat)) {
  dta_cmfts_40_test_output_concat[[col]][is.infinite(dta_cmfts_40_test_output_concat[[col]]) ] <- NA
}

set.seed(12345)

# Copy dta_cmfts_output_concat
dta_cmfts_40_test_output_imputed <- dta_cmfts_40_test_output_concat

# Get all numeric columns into a matrix
tmp <- as.matrix(dta_cmfts_40_test_output_imputed[, sapply(dta_cmfts_40_test_output_imputed, is.numeric)])
tmp[!is.finite(tmp)] <- NA

# Run missForest
impute_res <- missForest::missForest(tmp,
                         ntree = 100,
                         maxiter = 10,
                         verbose = TRUE)

dta_cmfts_40_test_output_imputed[, colnames(tmp)] <- impute_res$ximp

impute_res$OOBerror
```

NRMSE = 0.002

Features with zero SD:

```{r}
sds_zerosd <- sapply(dta_cmfts_40_test_output_imputed, function(x) {
  if(is.numeric(x)) sd(x, na.rm=T) else NA
})

# List features that do not meet the threshold
names(sds_zerosd)[is.na(sds_zerosd) | (sds_zerosd == 0)]

# Drop features with zero SD
dta_cmfts_40_test_output_imputed_zerosd <- dta_cmfts_40_test_output_imputed[, names(sds_zerosd)[is.na(sds_zerosd) | (sds_zerosd > 0)]]
```

The final dataset for this is ready: `dta_cmfts_40_test_output_imputed_zerosd`.

## Model Predictions

First to refine the test set so that it only has the relevant features.

```{r}
# Test set
dta_cmfts_40_test_output_imputed_zerosd$isfakeorreal <- factor(
  dta_cmfts_40_test_output_imputed_zerosd$isfakeorreal,
  levels = c("real", "fake")
)

dta_classifier_40_test_new <- dta_cmfts_40_test_output_imputed_zerosd %>%
  select(video_filename, emotion, isfakeorreal, all_of(boruta_selected_features))

dta_classifier_40_test_new_all <- dta_cmfts_40_test_output_imputed_zerosd %>%
  select(video_filename, emotion, isfakeorreal, all_of(all_component_features))

dta_classifier_40_test_new$video_filename <- dta_cmfts_40_test_output_imputed_zerosd$video_filename

head(dta_classifier_40_test_new_all)
```

### RF Model 1

No CV:

```{r}
rf_model_1_40_performance <- caret::confusionMatrix(
  data = predict(rf_fit_model1, newdata = dta_classifier_40_test_new),
  reference = dta_classifier_40_test_new$isfakeorreal,
  positive = "fake"
)

print(rf_model_1_40_performance)
```

Accuracy = 42.50%

95% CI = (0.2704, 0.5911)

NIR = 0.5

P = 0.8659

Kappa = -0.15

Sensitivity = 45%

Specificity = 40%

After pair preservation & oversampling:

Accuracy = 45%

95% CI = (0.2926, 0.6151)

NIR = 0.5

P = 0.7852

Kappa = -0.1

Sensitivity = 40%

Specificity = 50%

With CV:

```{r}
rf_model_1_40_cv_performance <- caret::confusionMatrix(
  data = predict(rf_fit_model1_cv, newdata = dta_classifier_40_test_new),
  reference = dta_classifier_40_test_new$isfakeorreal,
  positive = "fake"
)

print(rf_model_1_40_cv_performance)
```

Accuracy = 45.00%

95% CI = (0.2926, 0.6151)

NIR = 0.5

P = 0.7852

Kappa = -0.1

Sensitivity = 40%

Specificity = 50%

### RF Model 2

No CV:

```{r}
rf_model_2_40_performance <- caret::confusionMatrix(
  data = predict(rf_fit_model2, newdata = dta_classifier_40_test_new_all),
  reference = dta_classifier_40_test_new_all$isfakeorreal,
  positive = "fake"
)

print(rf_model_2_40_performance)
```

Accuracy = 57.50%

95% CI = (0.4089, 0.7296)

NIR = 0.5

P = 0.2148

Kappa = 0.15

Sensitivity = 75%

Specificity = 40%

After pair preservation & oversampling:

Accuracy = 60%

95% CI = (0.4333, 0.7514)

NIR = 0.5

P = 0.1341

Kappa = 0.2

Sensitivity = 55%

Specificity = 65%

With CV:

```{r}
rf_model_2_40_cv_performance <- caret::confusionMatrix(
  data = predict(rf_fit_model2_cv, newdata = dta_classifier_40_test_new_all),
  reference = dta_classifier_40_test_new_all$isfakeorreal,
  positive = "fake"
)

print(rf_model_2_40_cv_performance)
```

Accuracy = 55%

95% CI = (0.3849, 0.7074)

NIR = 0.5

P = 0.3179

Kappa = 0.1

Sensitivity = 70%

Specificity = 40%

### C5.0B Model 1

No CV:

```{r}
c50_model_1_40_performance <- caret::confusionMatrix(
  data = predict(c50_fit_model1, newdata = dta_classifier_40_test_new),
  reference = dta_classifier_40_test_new$isfakeorreal,
  positive = "fake"
)

print(c50_model_1_40_performance)
```

Accuracy = 50.00%

95% CI = (0.338, 0.662)

NIR = 0.5

P = 0.5627

Kappa = 0

Sensitivity = 90%

Specificity = 10%

After pair preservation & oversampling:\
Accuracy = 50.00%

95% CI = (0.338, 0.662)

NIR = 0.5

P = 0.5627

Kappa = 0

Sensitivity = 0%

Specificity = 100%

With CV:

```{r}
c50_model_1_40_cv_performance <- caret::confusionMatrix(
  data = predict(c50_fit_model1_cv, newdata = dta_classifier_40_test_new),
  reference = dta_classifier_40_test_new$isfakeorreal,
  positive = "fake"
)

print(c50_model_1_40_cv_performance)
```

Same as before.

### C5.0B Model 2

No CV:

```{r}
c50_model_2_40_performance <- caret::confusionMatrix(
  data = predict(c50_fit_model2, newdata = dta_classifier_40_test_new_all),
  reference = dta_classifier_40_test_new_all$isfakeorreal,
  positive = "fake"
)

print(c50_model_2_40_performance)
```

Same as before.

After pair preservation & oversampling:

Accuracy = 50.00%

95% CI = (0.338, 0.662)

NIR = 0.5

P = 0.5627

Kappa = 0

Sensitivity = 40%

Specificity = 60%

With CV:

```{r}
c50_model_2_40_cv_performance <- caret::confusionMatrix(
  data = predict(c50_fit_model2_cv, newdata = dta_classifier_40_test_new_all),
  reference = dta_classifier_40_test_new_all$isfakeorreal,
  positive = "fake"
)

print(c50_model_2_40_cv_performance)
```

Same as before.

# Emotion Vs No Emotion

## 20% Test Set:

### RF Model 1

Emotion:

```{r}
dta_classifier_test_new_emotion <- dta_classifier_test_new %>%
  filter(emotion == "yes")

rf_model_1_emotion <- caret::confusionMatrix(
  data = predict(rf_fit_model1, newdata = dta_classifier_test_new_emotion),
  reference = dta_classifier_test_new_emotion$isfakeorreal,
  positive = "fake"
)

print("--- Emotion: ---")
print(rf_model_1_emotion)
```

Accuracy = 52.63%

95% CI = (0.3897, 0.6602)

NIR = 0.5263

P = 0.5536

Kappa = 0.0587

Sensitivity = 59.26%

Specificity = 46.67%

After pair preservation & oversampling:

Accuracy = 63.79%

95% CI = (0.5012, 0.7601)

NIR = 0.5

P = 0.02395 (P \< 0.05)

Kappa = 0.276

Sensitivity = 79.31%

Specificity = 48.28%

No emotion:

```{r}
dta_classifier_test_new_no_emotion <- dta_classifier_test_new %>%
  filter(emotion == "no")

rf_model_1_no_emotion <- caret::confusionMatrix(
  data = predict(rf_fit_model1, newdata = dta_classifier_test_new_no_emotion),
  reference = dta_classifier_test_new_no_emotion$isfakeorreal,
  positive = "fake"
)

print("--- No Emotion: ---")
print(rf_model_1_no_emotion)
```

Accuracy = 36.11%

95% CI = (0.2082, 0.5378)

NIR = 0.6389

P = 0.9998

Kappa = -0.2395

Sensitivity = 38.46%

Specificity = 34.78%

After pair preservation & oversampling:

Accuracy = 61.11%

95% CI = (0.4346, 0.7686)

NIR = 0.5

P = 0.1215

Kappa = 0.2222

Sensitivity = 50%

Specificity = 72.22%

### RF Model 2

Emotion:

```{r}
dta_classifier_test_new_all_emotion <- dta_classifier_test_new_all %>%
  filter(emotion == "yes")

rf_model_2_emotion <- caret::confusionMatrix(
  data = predict(rf_fit_model2, newdata = dta_classifier_test_new_all_emotion),
  reference = dta_classifier_test_new_all_emotion$isfakeorreal,
  positive = "fake"
)

print("--- Emotion: ---")
print(rf_model_2_emotion)
```

Accuracy = 59.65%

95% CI = (0.4582, 0.7244)

NIR = 0.5263

P = 0.1767

Kappa = 0.2011

Sensitivity = 70.37%

Specificity = 50%

After pair preservation & oversampling:

Accuracy = 58.62%

95% CI = (0.4493, 0.714)

NIR = 0.5

P = 0.1185

Kappa = 0.1724

Sensitivity = 72.41%

Specificity = 44.83%

No Emotion:

```{r}
dta_classifier_test_new_all_no_emotion <- dta_classifier_test_new_all %>%
  filter(emotion == "no")

rf_model_2_no_emotion <- caret::confusionMatrix(
  data = predict(rf_fit_model2, newdata = dta_classifier_test_new_all_no_emotion),
  reference = dta_classifier_test_new_all_no_emotion$isfakeorreal,
  positive = "fake"
)

print("--- No Emotion: ---")
print(rf_model_2_no_emotion)
```

Accuracy = 47.22%

95% CI = (0.3041, 0.6451)

NIR = 0.6389

P = 0.9866

Kappa = 0.0604

Sensitivity = 76.92%

Specificity = 30.43%

After pair preservation & oversampling:

Accuracy = 58.33%

95% CI = (0.4076, 0.7449)

NIR = 0.5

P = 0.2025

Kappa = 0.1667

Sensitivity = 50%

Specificity = 66.67%

### C5.0B Model 1

Emotion:

```{r}
c50_model_1_emotion <- caret::confusionMatrix(
  data = predict(c50_fit_model1, newdata = dta_classifier_test_new_emotion),
  reference = dta_classifier_test_new_emotion$isfakeorreal,
  positive = "fake"
)

print("--- Emotion: ---")
print(c50_model_1_emotion)
```

Accuracy = 50.88%

95% CI = (0.3729, 0.6437)

NIR = 0.5263

P = 0.6552

Kappa = 0.0567

Sensitivity = 92.59%

Specificity = 13.33%

After pair preservation & oversampling:

Accuracy = 50%

95% CI = (0.3658, 0.6342)

NIR = 0.5

P = 0.5522

Kappa = 0

Sensitivity = 0%

Specificity = 100%

No Emotion:

```{r}
c50_model_1_no_emotion <- caret::confusionMatrix(
  data = predict(c50_fit_model1, newdata = dta_classifier_test_new_no_emotion),
  reference = dta_classifier_test_new_no_emotion$isfakeorreal,
  positive = "fake"
)

print("--- No Emotion: ---")
print(c50_model_1_no_emotion)
```

Accuracy = 38.89%

95% CI = (0.2314, 0.5654)

NIR = 0.6389

Kappa = 0.0318

Sensitivity = 100%

Specificity = 4.35%

### C5.0B Model 2

Emotion:

```{r}
c50_model_2_emotion <- caret::confusionMatrix(
  data = predict(c50_fit_model2, newdata = dta_classifier_test_new_all_emotion),
  reference = dta_classifier_test_new_all_emotion$isfakeorreal,
  positive = "fake"
)

print("--- Emotion: ---")
print(c50_model_2_emotion)
```

Before pair preservation and oversampling: Same as before.

No Emotion:

```{r}
c50_model_2_no_emotion <- caret::confusionMatrix(
  data = predict(c50_fit_model2, newdata = dta_classifier_test_new_all_no_emotion),
  reference = dta_classifier_test_new_all_no_emotion$isfakeorreal,
  positive = "fake"
)

print("--- No Emotion: ---")
print(c50_model_2_no_emotion)
```

## 40 Held-Out Videos:

### RF Model 1

Emotion:

```{r}
dta_classifier_40_test_new_emotion <- dta_classifier_40_test_new %>%
  filter(emotion == "y")

rf_model_1_40_emotion <- caret::confusionMatrix(
  data = predict(rf_fit_model1, newdata = dta_classifier_40_test_new_emotion),
  reference = dta_classifier_40_test_new_emotion$isfakeorreal,
  positive = "fake"
)

print("--- Emotion: ---")
print(rf_model_1_40_emotion)
```

Accuracy = 37.50%

95% CI = (0.152, 0.6457)

NIR = 0.5

P = 0.8949

Kappa = -0.25

Sensitivity = 50%

Specificity = 25%

No emotion:

```{r}
dta_classifier_40_test_new_no_emotion <- dta_classifier_40_test_new %>%
  filter(emotion == "n")

rf_model_1_40_no_emotion <- caret::confusionMatrix(
  data = predict(rf_fit_model1, newdata = dta_classifier_40_test_new_no_emotion),
  reference = dta_classifier_40_test_new_no_emotion$isfakeorreal,
  positive = "fake"
)

print("--- No Emotion: ---")
print(rf_model_1_40_no_emotion)
```

Accuracy = 45.83%

95% CI = (0.2555, 0.6718)

NIR = 0.5

P = 0.7294

Kappa = -0.0833

Sensitivity = 41.67%

Specificity = 50%

### RF Model 2

Emotion:

```{r}
dta_classifier_40_test_new_all_emotion <- dta_classifier_40_test_new_all %>%
  filter(emotion == "y")

rf_model_2_40_emotion <- caret::confusionMatrix(
  data = predict(rf_fit_model2, newdata = dta_classifier_40_test_new_all_emotion),
  reference = dta_classifier_40_test_new_all_emotion$isfakeorreal,
  positive = "fake"
)

print("--- Emotion: ---")
print(rf_model_2_40_emotion)
```

Accuracy = 43.75%

95% CI = (0.1975, 0.7012)

NIR = 0.5

P = 0.7728

Kappa = -0.125

Sensitivity = 62.50%

Specificity = 25%

No emotion:

```{r}
dta_classifier_40_test_new_all_no_emotion <- dta_classifier_40_test_new_all %>%
  filter(emotion == "n")

rf_model_2_40_no_emotion <- caret::confusionMatrix(
  data = predict(rf_fit_model2, newdata = dta_classifier_40_test_new_all_no_emotion),
  reference = dta_classifier_40_test_new_all_no_emotion$isfakeorreal,
  positive = "fake"
)

print("--- No Emotion: ---")
print(rf_model_2_40_no_emotion)
```

Accuracy = 62.50%

95% CI = (0.4059, 0.812)

NIR = 0.5

P = 0.1537

Kappa = 0.25

Sensitivity = 75%

Specificity = 50%

### C5.0B Model 1

Emotion:

```{r}
c50_model_1_40_emotion <- caret::confusionMatrix(
  data = predict(c50_fit_model1, newdata = dta_classifier_40_test_new_emotion),
  reference = dta_classifier_40_test_new_emotion$isfakeorreal,
  positive = "fake"
)

print("--- Emotion: ---")
print(c50_model_1_40_emotion)
```

Accuracy = 50.00%

95% CI = (0.2465, 07535)

NIR = 0.5

P = 0.5982

Kappa = 0

Sensitivity = 1

Specificity = 0

No emotion:

```{r}
c50_model_1_40_no_emotion <- caret::confusionMatrix(
  data = predict(c50_fit_model1, newdata = dta_classifier_40_test_new_no_emotion),
  reference = dta_classifier_40_test_new_no_emotion$isfakeorreal,
  positive = "fake"
)

print("--- No Emotion: ---")
print(c50_model_1_40_no_emotion)
```

Accuracy = 50.00%

95% CI = (0.2912, 0.7088)

NIR = 0.5

P = 0.5806

Kappa = 0

Sensitivity = 83.33%

Specificity = 16.67%

### C5.0B Model 2

Emotion:

```{r}
c50_model_2_40_emotion <- caret::confusionMatrix(
  data = predict(c50_fit_model2, newdata = dta_classifier_40_test_new_all_emotion),
  reference = dta_classifier_40_test_new_all_emotion$isfakeorreal,
  positive = "fake"
)

print("--- Emotion: ---")
print(c50_model_2_40_emotion)
```

# Normalising Features

Since we want to train additional non-decision tree models like SVM and PCA, we will need to normalise the features in both the train and test set using the parameters learned from the training dataset only. Doing it this way prevents data leakage.

```{r}
# Model 1
scaling_params_model1 <- list()
for (feature in names(dta_classifier_train_new)[-c(1:3)]) {
  scaling_params_model1[[feature]] <- list(
    mean = mean(dta_classifier_train_new[[feature]], na.rm = T),
    sd = sd(dta_classifier_train_new[[feature]], na.rm = T)
  )
}

# Model 2
scaling_params_model2 <- list()
for (feature in names(dta_classifier_train_new_all)[-c(1:3)]) {
  scaling_params_model2[[feature]] <- list(
    mean = mean(dta_classifier_train_new_all[[feature]], na.rm = T),
    sd = sd(dta_classifier_train_new_all[[feature]], na.rm = T)
  )
}
```

Now to apply to both training and test sets.

```{r}
# Create new instance of dataframes
dta_classifier_train_new_scaled <- dta_classifier_train_new
dta_classifier_test_new_scaled <- dta_classifier_test_new

dta_classifier_train_new_all_scaled <- dta_classifier_train_new_all
dta_classifier_test_new_all_scaled <- dta_classifier_test_new_all

# Model 1
for (feature in names(dta_classifier_train_new)[-c(1:3)]) {
  mean_val <- scaling_params_model1[[feature]]$mean
  sd_val <- scaling_params_model1[[feature]]$sd
```
