```{r}
  dta_classifier_train_new_scaled[[feature]] <- (dta_classifier_train_new[[feature]] - mean_val) / sd_val
  dta_classifier_test_new_scaled[[feature]] <- (dta_classifier_test_new[[feature]] - mean_val) / sd_val
}

# Model 2
for (feature in names(dta_classifier_train_new_all)[-c(1:3)]) {
  mean_val <- scaling_params_model2[[feature]]$mean
  sd_val <- scaling_params_model2[[feature]]$sd
  
  dta_classifier_train_new_all_scaled[[feature]] <- (dta_classifier_train_new_all[[feature]] - mean_val) / sd_val
  dta_classifier_test_new_all_scaled[[feature]] <- (dta_classifier_test_new_all[[feature]] - mean_val) / sd_val
}
```

Apply to 40 held out videos:

```{r}
# Create new instance of dataframes
dta_classifier_40_test_new_scaled <- dta_classifier_40_test_new
dta_classifier_40_test_new_all_scaled <- dta_classifier_40_test_new_all

# Model 1
for (feature in names(dta_classifier_40_test_new)[-c(1:3)]) {
  mean_val <- scaling_params_model1[[feature]]$mean
  sd_val <- scaling_params_model1[[feature]]$sd
  
  dta_classifier_40_test_new_scaled[[feature]] <- (dta_classifier_40_test_new[[feature]] - mean_val) / sd_val
}

# Model 2
for (feature in names(dta_classifier_40_test_new_all)[-c(1:3)]) {
  mean_val <- scaling_params_model2[[feature]]$mean
  sd_val <- scaling_params_model2[[feature]]$sd
  
  dta_classifier_40_test_new_all_scaled[[feature]] <- (dta_classifier_40_test_new_all[[feature]] - mean_val) / sd_val
}
```

Test to see if decision tree model performance differs (RF Model 1):

```{r}
# Cross validation setup
classifier_control <- caret::trainControl(
  method = "none",
  classProbs = TRUE,
  summaryFunction = caret::twoClassSummary
)

set.seed(12345)

rf_fit_model1_scaled <- caret::train(
  isfakeorreal ~ ., # nmf
  data = dta_classifier_train_new_scaled[, 3:4],
  method = "rf",
  metric = "ROC",
  trControl = classifier_control,
  tuneGrid = expand.grid(mtry = 4)
)

print(rf_fit_model1_scaled)

rf_model_1_scaled_performance <- caret::confusionMatrix(
  data = predict(rf_fit_model1_scaled, newdata = dta_classifier_test_new_scaled),
  reference = dta_classifier_test_new_scaled$isfakeorreal,
  positive = "fake"
)

print(rf_model_1_scaled_performance)

```

-   Accuracy = 62.77%

-   95% CI = (0.5218, 0.7252)

-   NIR = 0.5

-   P = 0.008605 (P \< .01)

-   Kappa = 0.2553

-   Sensitivity = 68.09%

-   Specificity = 57.45%

-   Precision = 61.54%

-   F1 = 64.65%

Performance is slightly worse than before normalisation but is roughly the same, suggesting the signal is mostly preserved.

# Additional Models

It is a good idea to train a few more classifiers (non-decision tree) to see how they compare to the current decision tree models. This allows us to see whether there is a significant difference between decision tree and non-decision tree classifiers.

## SVM

Support vector machines are a good choice as they are also non-linear but operate in a different way to decision trees (geometrically finds the optimal hyperplane that maximises the margin between different classes).

### Model 1

```{r}
# Cross validation setup
classifier_control <- caret::trainControl(
  method = "none",
  classProbs = TRUE,
  summaryFunction = caret::twoClassSummary
)

svm_model1 <- caret::train(
  isfakeorreal ~ .,
  data = dta_classifier_train_new_scaled[, 3:4],
  method = "svmRadial",  # RBF kernel
  trControl = classifier_control
)
```

Evaluate on 20% test set:

```{r}
svm_model_1_performance <- caret::confusionMatrix(
  data = predict(svm_model1, newdata = dta_classifier_test_new_scaled),
  reference = dta_classifier_test_new_scaled$isfakeorreal,
  positive = "fake"
)

print(svm_model_1_performance)
```

-   Accuracy = 50%

-   95% CI = (0.3951, 0.6049)

-   NIR = 0.5

-   P = 0.5410

-   Kappa = 0

-   Sensitivity = 40.43%

-   Specificity = 59.57%

-   Precision = 50%

-   F1 = 44.71%

Evaluate on 40 held out videos:

```{r}
svm_model_1_40_performance <- caret::confusionMatrix(
  data = predict(svm_model1, newdata = dta_classifier_40_test_new_scaled),
  reference = dta_classifier_40_test_new_scaled$isfakeorreal,
  positive = "fake"
)

print(svm_model_1_40_performance)
```

-   Accuracy = 55%

-   95% CI = (0.3849, 0.7074)

-   NIR = 0.5

-   P = 0.317914

-   Kappa = 0.1

-   Sensitivity = 25%

-   Specificity = 85%

-   Precision = 62.50%

-   F1 = 35.71%

### Model 2

```{r}
# Cross validation setup
classifier_control <- caret::trainControl(
  method = "none",
  classProbs = TRUE,
  summaryFunction = caret::twoClassSummary
)

svm_model2 <- caret::train(
  isfakeorreal ~ .,
  data = dta_classifier_train_new_all_scaled[, 3:6],
  method = "svmRadial",  # RBF kernel
  trControl = classifier_control
)
```

Evaluate on test set:

```{r}
svm_model_2_performance <- caret::confusionMatrix(
  data = predict(svm_model2, newdata = dta_classifier_test_new_all_scaled),
  reference = dta_classifier_test_new_all_scaled$isfakeorreal,
  positive = "fake"
)

print(svm_model_2_performance)
```

Accuracy = 58.51%

95% CI = (0.4788, 0.6859)

NIR = 0.5

P = 0.06069

Kappa = 0.1702

Sensitivity = 55.32%

Specificity = 61.70%

Precision = 59.09%

F1 = 57.14%

Evaluate on 40 held out videos:

```{r}
svm_model_2_40_performance <- caret::confusionMatrix(
  data = predict(svm_model2, newdata = dta_classifier_40_test_new_all_scaled),
  reference = dta_classifier_40_test_new_all_scaled$isfakeorreal,
  positive = "fake"
)

print(svm_model_2_40_performance)
```

-   Accuracy = 52.50%

-   95% CI = (0.3613, 0.6849)

-   NIR = 0.5

-   P = 0.43731

-   Kappa = 0.05

-   Sensitivity = 30%

-   Specificity = 75%

-   Precision - 54.55%

-   F1 = 38.71%

## Linear Regression

This will be the linear benchmark to compare our other non-linear model performances to. A comparable performance might suggest that the data is not non-linear and is best captured by linear models.

### Model 1

```{r}
# Cross validation setup
classifier_control <- caret::trainControl(
  method = "none",
  classProbs = TRUE,
  summaryFunction = caret::twoClassSummary
)

lr_model1 <- caret::train(
  isfakeorreal ~ .,
  data = dta_classifier_train_new_scaled[, 3:4],
  method = "glm",  # Generalised linear models
  family = "binomial",
  trControl = classifier_control
)
```

Evaluate on test set:

```{r}
lr_model1_performance <- caret::confusionMatrix(
  data = predict(lr_model1, newdata = dta_classifier_test_new_scaled),
  reference = dta_classifier_test_new_scaled$isfakeorreal,
  positive = "fake"
)

print(lr_model1_performance)
```

-   Accuracy = 50%

-   95% CI = (0.3951, 0.6049)

-   NIR = 0.5

-   P = 0.541

-   Kappa = 0

-   Sensitivity = 21.28%

-   Specificity = 78.72%

-   Precision = 50%

-   F1 = 29.85%

Evaluate on 40 held out videos:

```{r}
lr_model1_40_performance <- caret::confusionMatrix(
  data = predict(lr_model1, newdata = dta_classifier_40_test_new_scaled),
  reference = dta_classifier_40_test_new_scaled$isfakeorreal,
  positive = "fake"
)

print(lr_model1_40_performance)
```

-   Accuracy = 47.50%

-   95% CI = (0.3151, 0.6387)

-   NIR = 0.5

-   P = 0.68209

-   Kappa = -0.05

-   Sensitivity = 10%

-   Specificity = 85%

-   Precision = 40%

-   F1 = 16%

### Model 2

```{r}
# Cross validation setup
classifier_control <- caret::trainControl(
  method = "none",
  classProbs = TRUE,
  summaryFunction = caret::twoClassSummary
)

lr_model2 <- caret::train(
  isfakeorreal ~ .,
  data = dta_classifier_train_new_all_scaled[, 3:6],
  method = "glm",  # Generalised linear models
  family = "binomial",
  trControl = classifier_control
)
```

Evaluate on test set:

```{r}
lr_model2_performance <- caret::confusionMatrix(
  data = predict(lr_model2, newdata = dta_classifier_test_new_all_scaled),
  reference = dta_classifier_test_new_all_scaled$isfakeorreal,
  positive = "fake"
)

print(lr_model2_performance)
```

-   Accuracy = 53.19%

-   95% CI = (0.4261, 0.6356)

-   NIR = 0.5

-   P = 0.3031

-   Kappa = 0.0638

-   Sensitivity = 42.55%

-   Specificity = 63.83%

-   Precision = 54.05%

-   F1 = 47.62%

Evaluate on 40 held out videos:

```{r}
lr_model2_40_performance <- caret::confusionMatrix(
  data = predict(lr_model2, newdata = dta_classifier_40_test_new_all_scaled),
  reference = dta_classifier_40_test_new_all_scaled$isfakeorreal,
  positive = "fake"
)

print(lr_model2_40_performance)
```

-   Accuracy = 55%

-   95% CI = (0.3849, 0.7074)

-   NIR = 0.5

-   P = 0.31791

-   Kappa = 0.1

-   Sensitivity = 35%

-   Specificity = 75%

-   Precision = 58.33%

-   F1 = 43.75%

# PCA

Will now use PCA as an alternative to feature selection. The hypothesis here is that combinations of features captured by PCA might provide a more stable and generalisable signal for the models to learn from. This is due to the poor model performance across the board using current feature selection methods.

```{r}
set.seed(12345)

train_predictors_pca <- dta_cmfts_emotion_balanced %>%
  select(where(is.numeric))

# Create PCA transformation object from training data
# Center, scale and then perform PCA
pca_transform <- caret::preProcess(
  train_predictors_pca,
  method = c("center", "scale", "pca"),
  thresh = 0.95  # Retain components explaining 95% of variance
)

print(pca_transform)
```

PCA needed 51 components to capture 95% of the variance.

This suggests that the variance in the data is widely distributed. There isn't a small, neat set of underlying dimensions that explain most of the information. Instead, the signal is spread thinly across many of the original features. This suggests the patterns are complex and noisy.

This could lead to risk of overfitting, since 51 is a high number of predictors to feed into a model. PCA as a whole also leads to reduced interpretability as it is impossible to look at the 51 different components and interpret what they represent in a meaningful way.

```{r}
# Load data
dta_preprocessed <- read.csv("../../CSV files//OpenFace files/full_dataset_preprocessed.csv")

dta_nmf_input %>%
  subset(video_filename %in% c("19__talking_against_wall", "24__talking_against_wall", "19_24__talking_against_wall__QD5K7BP2")) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = starts_with("AU"),
    names_to = "AU",
    values_to = "AU_values"
  ) %>%
  subset(AU == "AU06_cheek_raiser") %>%
  mutate(AU_difference = lead(AU_values) - AU_values) %>%
  ggplot(aes(frame, AU_values)) +
  #geom_line(aes(group = AU)) +
  geom_line(aes(y = AU_difference), colour = "blue") +
  facet_grid(~ video_filename)

# unique(dta_nmf_input$video_filename)

dta_preprocessed %>%
  subset(video_filename %in% c("21__outside_talking_still_laughing", "14__outside_talking_still_laughing", "21_14__outside_talking_still_laughing__IRKML4J0")) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = starts_with("AU"),
    names_to = "AU",
    values_to = "AU_values"
  ) %>%
  subset(AU == "AU06_r") %>%
  mutate(AU_difference = lead(AU_values) - AU_values) %>%
  ggplot(aes(frame, AU_values)) +
  #geom_line(aes(group = AU)) +
  geom_line(aes(y = AU_difference), colour = "blue") +
  facet_grid(~ video_filename)


dta_nmf_input %>%
  #subset(video_filename %in% c("19__talking_against_wall", "24__talking_against_wall", "19_24__talking_against_wall__QD5K7BP2")) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = starts_with("AU"),
    names_to = "AU",
    values_to = "AU_values"
  ) %>%
  subset(AU == "AU06_cheek_raiser") %>%
  mutate(AU_difference = lead(AU_values) - AU_values) %>%
  ggplot(aes(frame, AU_values)) +
  #geom_line(aes(group = AU)) +
  geom_smooth(aes(group = emotion, y = AU_difference), colour = "blue")
  #facet_grid(~ emotion)
```

```{r}
# NMF Components

dta_nmf_output %>%
  subset(video_filename %in% c("28__kitchen_still", "08__kitchen_still", "28_08__kitchen_still__HIOU8Z8I")) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = starts_with("nmf"),
    names_to = "nmf",
    values_to = "nmf_values"
  ) %>%
  #subset(nmf == "nmf2") %>%
  mutate(nmf_difference = lead(nmf_values) - nmf_values) %>%
  ggplot(aes(frame, nmf_values)) +
  #geom_line(aes(group = AU)) +
  geom_line(aes(y = nmf_difference), colour = "blue") +
  facet_grid(nmf ~ video_filename)
```

## Visualise PCA Components

```{r, fig.width = 6, fig.height = 4}
pca_results <- prcomp(train_predictors_pca, scale. = TRUE)

# Calculate variance explained by each comp
variance_explained <- (pca_results$sdev^2) / sum(pca_results$sdev^2)
cumulative_variance <- cumsum(variance_explained)

# Create df for plot
dta_pca_scree <- data.frame(
  Component = 1:length(variance_explained),
  Variance = variance_explained,
  CumulativeVariance = cumulative_variance
)

# plot
dta_pca_scree %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = Component)) +
  geom_col(aes(y = Variance), fill = "steelblue", alpha = 0.8) +
  theme_bw()


# Plot with cumulative variance
plt_pca_scree <- dta_pca_scree %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = Component)) +
  geom_col(aes(y = Variance), fill = "steelblue", alpha = 0.8) +
  geom_line(aes(y = CumulativeVariance), colour = "red3", size = 1) +
  geom_point(aes(y = CumulativeVariance), colour = "red3", size = 1) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.95, linetype = "dashed", colour = "black") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 25, linetype = "dashed", colour = "darkgreen") +
  labs(
    title = "PCA Scree Plot",
    x = "Principal Component",
    y = "Proportion of Variance Explained"
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    name = "Proportion of Variance Explained (Bars)",
    sec.axis = sec_axis(~., name = "Cumulative Proportion of Variance (Line)")
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    axis.title.y.left = element_text(colour = "steelblue"),
    axis.title.y.right = element_text(colour = "red3"),
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5)
  )

plt_pca_scree

ggsave(
  filename = "plt_pca_scree.pdf",
  plot = plt_pca_scree,
  height = 4,
  width = 6,
  device = cairo_pdf
)
```

We can see that the component explaining the most variance is still quite small (just over 10%). We can also see that it takes 7 PCs to explain over 50% variance.

We can use the eigenvalues greater than 1 rule (Kaiser criterion), which suggests keeping components that explain more variance than a single one of the original standardised variables.

```{r}
cat("Number of components with eigenvalues > 1:", sum(pca_results$sdev^2 > 1))
```

25 components have an eigenvalue greater than 1.

I will try 25 components first.

## Get PCA Features for Model Training

```{r}
pca_transform_25 <- caret::preProcess(
  train_predictors_pca,
  method = c("center", "scale", "pca"),
  pcaComp = 25
)

dta_train_pca_25 <- predict(pca_transform_25, train_predictors_pca)
dta_test_pca_25 <- predict(pca_transform_25,
                              dta_cmfts_test_output_imputed_zerosd %>%
                                select(where(is.numeric)))

dta_test_40_pca_25 <- predict(pca_transform_25,
                              dta_cmfts_40_test_output_imputed_zerosd %>%
                                select(where(is.numeric)))

dta_train_pca_25 <- dta_cmfts_emotion_balanced %>%
  select(video_filename, emotion, isfakeorreal) %>%
  bind_cols(dta_train_pca_25)

dta_test_pca_25 <- dta_cmfts_test_output_imputed_zerosd %>%
  select(video_filename, emotion, isfakeorreal) %>%
  bind_cols(dta_test_pca_25)

dta_test_40_pca_25 <- dta_cmfts_40_test_output_imputed_zerosd %>%
  select(video_filename, emotion, isfakeorreal) %>%
  bind_cols(dta_test_40_pca_25)
```

Train set: `dta_test_pca_25`

Test set: `dta_test_pca_25`

40 held out videos: `dta_test_40_pca_25`

## Classifiers (25 Components)

### RF

```{r}
# Cross validation setup
classifier_control <- caret::trainControl(
  method = "none",
  classProbs = TRUE,
  summaryFunction = caret::twoClassSummary
)

set.seed(12345)

rf_fit_model1_pca_25 <- caret::train(
  isfakeorreal ~ ., # nmf
  data = dta_train_pca_25[, -c(1:2)],
  method = "rf",
  metric = "ROC",
  trControl = classifier_control,
  tuneGrid = expand.grid(mtry = 4)
)

print(rf_fit_model1_pca_25)
plot(caret::varImp(rf_fit_model1_pca_25)) # To see feature importance
```

Evaluate on test sets:

```{r}
rf_model_1_pca_25_performance <- caret::confusionMatrix(
  data = predict(rf_fit_model1_pca_25, newdata = dta_test_pca_25),
  reference = dta_test_pca_25$isfakeorreal,
  positive = "fake"
)

print(rf_model_1_pca_25_performance)
```

-   Accuracy = 55.32%

-   95% CI = (0.4471, 0.6559)

-   NIR = 0.5

-   P = 0.1767

-   Kappa = 0.1064

-   Sensitivity = 48.94%

-   Specificity = 61.70%

-   Precision = 56.10%

-   F1 = 52.28%

40 held out videos:

```{r}
rf_model_1_40_pca_25_performance <- caret::confusionMatrix(
  data = predict(rf_fit_model1_pca_25, newdata = dta_test_40_pca_25),
  reference = dta_test_40_pca_25$isfakeorreal,
  positive = "fake"
)

print(rf_model_1_40_pca_25_performance)
```

-   Accuracy = 55%

-   95% CI = (0.3849, 0.7074)

-   NIR = 0.5

-   P = 0.3179

-   Kappa = 0.1

-   Sensitivity = 45%

-   Specificity = 65%

-   Precision = 56.25%

-   F1 = 50%

### C5.0B

```{r}
set.seed(12345)

c50_fit_model1_pca_25 <- caret::train(
  isfakeorreal ~ .,
  data = dta_train_pca_25[, -c(1:2)],
  method = "C5.0",
  metric = "ROC",
  trControl = classifier_control,
  tuneGrid = expand.grid(
    model = "tree",
    trials = 20,  # number of boosting iterations
    winnow = FALSE
  )
)

print(c50_fit_model1_pca_25)
plot(caret::varImp(c50_fit_model1_pca_25)) # To see feature importanc
```

Evaluate on test data:

```{r}
c50_model_1_performance_pca_25 <- caret::confusionMatrix(
  data = predict(c50_fit_model1_pca_25, newdata = dta_test_pca_25),
  reference = dta_test_pca_25$isfakeorreal,
  positive = "fake"
)

print(c50_model_1_performance_pca_25)
```

-   Accuracy = 58.51%

-   95% CI = (0.4788, 0.6859)

-   NIR = 0.5

-   P = 0.06069

-   Kappa = 0.1702

-   Sensitivity = 42.55%

-   Specificity = 74.47%

-   Precision = 62.50%

-   F1 = 50.63%

Evaluate on 40 held out videos:

```{r}
c50_model_1_40_performance_pca_25 <- caret::confusionMatrix(
  data = predict(c50_fit_model1_pca_25, newdata = dta_test_40_pca_25),
  reference = dta_test_40_pca_25$isfakeorreal,
  positive = "fake"
)

print(c50_model_1_40_performance_pca_25)
```

-   Accuracy = 45%

-   95% CI = (0.2926, 0.6151)

-   NIR = 0.5

-   P = 0.7852

-   Kappa = -0.1

-   Sensitivity = 40%

-   Specificity = 50%

-   Precision = 44.44%

-   F1 = 42.10%

### SVM

```{r}
# Cross validation setup
classifier_control <- caret::trainControl(
  method = "none",
  classProbs = TRUE,
  summaryFunction = caret::twoClassSummary
)

svm_model1_pca_25 <- caret::train(
  isfakeorreal ~ .,
  data = dta_train_pca_25[, -c(1:2)],
  method = "svmRadial",  # RBF kernel
  trControl = classifier_control
)
```

Evaluate on test data:

```{r}
svm_model_1_pca_25_performance <- caret::confusionMatrix(
  data = predict(svm_model1_pca_25, newdata = dta_test_pca_25),
  reference = dta_test_pca_25$isfakeorreal,
  positive = "fake"
)

print(svm_model_1_pca_25_performance)
```

-   Accuracy = 55.32%

-   95% CI = (0.4471, 0.6559)

-   NIR = 0.5

-   P = 0.1767

-   Kappa = 0.1064

-   Sensitivity = 57.54%

-   Specificity = 53.19%

-   Precision = 55.10%

-   F1 = 56.29%

Evaluate on 40 held out set:

```{r}
svm_model_1_40_pca_25_performance <- caret::confusionMatrix(
  data = predict(svm_model1_pca_25, newdata = dta_test_40_pca_25),
  reference = dta_test_40_pca_25$isfakeorreal,
  positive = "fake"
)

print(svm_model_1_40_pca_25_performance)
```

-   Accuracy = 50%

-   95% CI = (0.338, 0.662)

-   NIR = 0.5

-   P = 0.5627

-   Kappa = 0

-   Sensitivity = 60%

-   Specificity = 40%

-   Precision = 50%

-   F1 = 54.55%

### Logistic Regression

```{r}
# Cross validation setup
classifier_control <- caret::trainControl(
  method = "none",
  classProbs = TRUE,
  summaryFunction = caret::twoClassSummary
)

lr_model1_pca_25 <- caret::train(
  isfakeorreal ~ .,
  data = dta_train_pca_25[, -c(1:2)],
  method = "glm",  # Generalised linear models
  family = "binomial",
  trControl = classifier_control
)
```

Evaluate on test set:

```{r}
lr_model1_pca_25_performance <- caret::confusionMatrix(
  data = predict(lr_model1_pca_25, newdata = dta_test_pca_25),
  reference = dta_test_pca_25$isfakeorreal,
  positive = "fake"
)

print(lr_model1_pca_25_performance)
```

-   Accuracy = 55.32%

-   95% CI = (0.4471, 0.6559)

-   NIR = 0.5

-   P = 0.1767

-   Kappa = 0.1064

-   Sensitivity = 51.06%

-   Specificity = 59.57%

-   Precision = 55.81%

-   F1 = 53.33%

Evaluate on 40 held out videos:

```{r}
lr_model1_40_pca_25_performance <- caret::confusionMatrix(
  data = predict(lr_model1_pca_25, newdata = dta_test_40_pca_25),
  reference = dta_test_40_pca_25$isfakeorreal,
  positive = "fake"
)

print(lr_model1_40_pca_25_performance)
```

-   Accuracy = 47.50%

-   95% CI = (0.3151, 0.6387)

-   NIR = 0.5

-   P = 0.6821

-   Kappa = -0.05

-   Sensitivity = 40%

-   Specificity = 55%

-   Precision = 47.06%

-   F1 = 43.24%

# Reconstruction Pt2

```{r}
# ---------- 1.  PER‑AU  R²  FOR  BOTH  TEST  SETS ----------

# helper to compute R² column‑wise
r2_vec <- function(orig_mat, recon_mat) {
  vapply(seq_along(au_names), function(j) {
    y  <- orig_mat[ , j]
    ŷ <- recon_mat[ , j]
    1 - sum((y - ŷ)^2) / sum((y - mean(y))^2)
  }, numeric(1))
}

r2_test20 <- r2_vec(project_V_orig,       project_V_recon)      # 20% split
r2_test40 <- r2_vec(project_40_V_orig,    project_40_V_recon)   # 40 videos

# ---------- 2.  COMBINE  &  SHOW  AS  TABLE ----------
dta_r2 <- tibble::tibble(
  AU          = au_names,
  R2_test20   = round(r2_test20, 3),
  R2_test40   = round(r2_test40, 3)
) %>% dplyr::arrange(dplyr::desc(R2_test20))

knitr::kable(
  dta_r2,
  caption = "Per‑AU reconstruction R² for the 20% test split and 40‑video held‑out set"
)

```

We can see that the highest R2 value is for AU07 across both test sets (20% test set = 0.887, 40 videos = 0.899).

The lowest R2 value is for AU23 across both test sets (20% test set = 0.010, 40 videos = 0.011).

```{r, fig.width=6, fig.height=4}
# ---------- 1.  IDENTIFY  BEST & WORST  (using 20% test set) ----------
best_au  <- dta_r2$AU[ which.max(dta_r2$R2_test20) ]
worst_au <- dta_r2$AU[ which.min(dta_r2$R2_test20) ]

# choose one representative test‑set video for the time‑series plot
target_video <- dplyr::first(unique(dta_nmf_input_test$video_filename))

# helper to build a tidy data frame for a given AU
build_plot_df <- function(target_au) {
  au_idx <- which(colnames(project_V_orig) == target_au)
  rows   <- which(dta_nmf_input_test$video_filename == target_video)
  
  tibble::tibble(
    frame        = dta_nmf_input_test$frame[rows],
    AU           = target_au,
    Original     = project_V_orig [rows, au_idx],
    Reconstructed= project_V_recon[rows, au_idx]
  ) |>
    tidyr::pivot_longer(
      cols      = c(Original, Reconstructed),
      names_to  = "Source",
      values_to = "Activation"
    )
}

plot_df <- dplyr::bind_rows(
  build_plot_df(best_au),
  build_plot_df(worst_au)
)

# ---------- 2.  PLOT ----------
ggplot2::ggplot(plot_df,
                ggplot2::aes(x = frame, y = Activation, colour = Source)) +
  ggplot2::geom_line(size = 0.5) +
  ggplot2::facet_wrap(~ AU, scales = "free_y") +
  ggplot2::theme_minimal() +
  ggplot2::labs(
    title = "Original vs. Reconstructed AUs",
    subtitle = paste0("Video: ", target_video),
    x     = "Frame", y = "AU activation"
  )

```

# Dominant AU Averaging

Additional check.

Instead of using complex CMFTS features, check to see if we can get similar results with a much simpler set of features. Will do this by creating 3 new features, using the normalised basis matrix to find dominant AUs for each component (above .5 threshold). Then for each video, calculate the average activation of only those dominant AUs, giving three new features. Then train RF model using these three features.

```{r}
# 1. Get the normalised basis matrix (W)
# This uses fn_maxnormalise() defined in your "Visualisations" section
norm_W <- apply(model_nmf$w, 2, fn_maxnormalise)

# Assign AU names (defined in your "Visualisations" section)
rownames(norm_W) <- au_names

print("Normalised Basis Matrix (W):")
print(norm_W)

# 2. Identify dominant AUs for each component
threshold <- 0.5
dominant_aus_list <- apply(norm_W, 2, function(col) {
  names(col[col > threshold])
})

# Rename for clarity
names(dominant_aus_list) <- c("nmf1_dominant_aus", "nmf2_dominant_aus", "nmf3_dominant_aus")

print("Dominant AUs for each component (>.5 threshold):")
print(dominant_aus_list)
```

Now we know which AUs are dominant for each component, we can create new features.

```{r}
# 1. Create new features for the TRAIN set
# First, get the average of each AU for each video
dta_train_au_avg <- dta_nmf_input_train %>%
  select(video_filename, isfakeorreal, emotion, all_of(au_names)) %>%
  group_by(video_filename, isfakeorreal, emotion) %>%
  summarise(across(all_of(au_names), mean, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  distinct() # Ensure one row per video

# 2. Calculate the new component-average features
dta_train_new_features <- dta_train_au_avg %>%
  mutate(
    # Use rowMeans to get the average of the dominant AU columns for that row
    # The 'select' helper function chooses the correct columns
    Comp1_Avg = rowMeans(select(., all_of(dominant_aus_list[[1]]))),
    Comp2_Avg = rowMeans(select(., all_of(dominant_aus_list[[2]]))),
    Comp3_Avg = rowMeans(select(., all_of(dominant_aus_list[[3]])))
  ) %>%
  select(video_filename, isfakeorreal, emotion, Comp1_Avg, Comp2_Avg, Comp3_Avg)

head(dta_train_new_features)
```

Create corresponding features for the test set.

```{r}
# 3. Create new features for the TEST set
# First, get the average of each AU for each video
dta_test_au_avg <- dta_nmf_input_test %>%
  select(video_filename, isfakeorreal, emotion, all_of(au_names)) %>%
  group_by(video_filename, isfakeorreal, emotion) %>%
  summarise(across(all_of(au_names), mean, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  distinct() # Ensure one row per video

# 4. Calculate the new component-average features
dta_test_new_features <- dta_test_au_avg %>%
  mutate(
    Comp1_Avg = rowMeans(select(., all_of(dominant_aus_list[[1]]))),
    Comp2_Avg = rowMeans(select(., all_of(dominant_aus_list[[2]]))),
    Comp3_Avg = rowMeans(select(., all_of(dominant_aus_list[[3]])))
  ) %>%
  select(video_filename, isfakeorreal, emotion, Comp1_Avg, Comp2_Avg, Comp3_Avg)

# Set outcome var as factor
dta_test_new_features$isfakeorreal <- factor(
  dta_test_new_features$isfakeorreal,
  levels = c("real", "fake")
)

head(dta_test_new_features)
```

Test the new features with RF with repeated CV:

```{r}
# Task 2: Test new features using Random Forest with CV
# Use the same CV setup as your previous "Repeated Cross-Validation" section
classifier_control_cv <- caret::trainControl(
  method = "repeatedcv",
  number = 5,
  repeats = 3,
  classProbs = TRUE,
  summaryFunction = caret::twoClassSummary
)

set.seed(12345)

# Train the model
rf_fit_new_features_cv <- caret::train(
  # Formula: predict isfakeorreal using only the 3 new features
  isfakeorreal ~ Comp1_Avg + Comp2_Avg + Comp3_Avg,
  data = dta_train_new_features,
  method = "rf",
  metric = "ROC",
  trControl = classifier_control_cv,
  tuneLength = 5 # Let caret search for a good mtry
)

# Print CV results
print(rf_fit_new_features_cv)

# See feature importance
plot(caret::varImp(rf_fit_new_features_cv))
```

Evaluate on test data:

```{r}
# Evaluate the new model on the unseen test set
rf_new_features_cv_performance <- caret::confusionMatrix(
  data = predict(rf_fit_new_features_cv, newdata = dta_test_new_features),
  reference = dta_test_new_features$isfakeorreal,
  positive = "fake"
)

print(rf_new_features_cv_performance)
```
